program_outcomes: Yönetim bilişim sistemlerine yönelik, başta işletme ve bilgisayar mühendisliği ile ilgili olmak üzere, disiplinlerarası geniş bir perspektife sahiptir.
Takım çalışması, müzakere, liderlik ve girişimcilik yeteneklerini geliştirir.
Evrensel etik değerlere, sosyal sorumluluk bilincine ve yeterli düzeyde gerekli hukuk bilgisine sahiptir.
Yönetim bilişim sistemlerini teknik, örgütsel ve yönetsel açıdan kavrar ve programlama mantığını bilerek güncel bir programlama dili kullanır.
Çeşitli iş problemlerinin kavranmasına ve çözümüne yönelik farklı bilişim teknolojileri ve sistemlerini kullanır.
Yönetim bilişim sistemleri alanındaki verileri, kavram ve fikirleri bilimsel ve teknolojik yöntemlerle yorumlar.
Çeşitli istatistiki teknikleri ve sayısal yöntemleri kullanarak karmaşık iş ve bilişim problemlerini çözer ve istatistik programlarını etkin bir şekilde kullanarak analizler yapar.
Alanının gerektirdiği en az Avrupa Bilgisayar Kullanma Lisansı ileri düzeyinde bilgisayar yazılımı ile birlikte bilişim ve iletişim teknolojilerini kullanır.
Bir yabancı dili eğitim-öğretim düzeyine göre, Avrupa Dil Portföyü kriteri açısından en az B1 Genel Düzeyi’nde kullanır.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmeye ilişkin olumlu tutumlar geliştirebilerek bireysel öğrenme ihtiyaçlarını belirler ve bunları gidermeye yönelik çalışmalar yapar.
Bir bilişim sistemi için gerekli ihtiyaçları analiz ederek sisteme ait veritabanın analiz, dizayn ve uygulama aşamalarındaki süreçlere hakim olur.
Alanı ile ilgili konularda düşünce ve çözüm önerilerini hem yazılı hem de sözlü olarak aktarır ve gerektiğinde hem ulusal hem de uluslararası platformlarda sunar ve yayınlar.
Bilişim projelerine teknik ve yönetsel katkı verir ve sorumluluk alır.

courses.name: Mathematics for Data Science 1
courses.purpose: Tek değişkenli fonksiyonlarda limit, süreklilik ve türev kavramlarını öğretmek; türev ve integral kavramlarını uygulamada kullanma becerisi sağlamak; matematik bilgisini mühendislik problemlerini çözmede kullanabilme becerisi kazandırmak.
courses.description: Giriş: reel sayılar ve reel doğru, doğrular, çemberler ve paraboler, fonksiyonlar ve grafikleri, belirli fonksiyonlar, matematik modeller, fonksiyon kuralları, öteleme kuralları ve trigonometrik fonksiyonlar. Limit ve süreklilik: değişim oranı ve limit, limit bulma ve limit kuralları, limit tanımı, tek yönlü limitler ve sonsuzda limit, sonsuz limit ve dikey asimptotlar, süreklilik, teğet ve türev. Türev: fonksiyon olarak türev, türev kuralları, değişim oranı olarak türev, trigonometrik fonksiyonların türevleri, zincir kuralı ve parametrik denklemler, kapalı türetme. Türev uygulamaları: fonksiyonların ekstrem değerleri, ortalama değer teoremi, monotonik fonksiyonlar ve birinci mertebe türev testi, konkavite ve eğri çizimi, belirsizlik durumları ve L’Hopital kuralı. İntegral: sonlu toplam ile hesaplama, sigma notasyonu ve sonlu toplam limiti, belirli integral, Calculus hesabın temel teoremi, belirsiz integraller ve değişken dönüşümü, iki eğri arasında kalan alan hesabı.
learning_outcomes: Tek değişkenli fonksiyonlarda limit ve süreklilik kavramlarını kullanabilme.
Fonksiyonların grafiğini, asimptot, kritik nokta, azalan/artan ve konkavlığını inceleyerek çizebilme.
Maksimum minimum problemlerini kurabilme ve optimizasyon problemlerini çözebilme.
Belirli integral hesabı ve belirli integral yardımıyla alan problemlerini çözebilme, değişken dönüşümü yardımıyla integral alma.
resources: Calculus, Early Transcendentals, 7th Edition, by J. Stewart, 2008, Brook/Cole
Thomas’ Calculus, 11th Edition, G.B Thomas, M.D.Weir, J.Hass and F.R.Giordano, Addison-Wesley, 2005.

courses.name: Introduction to AI
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental concepts, techniques, and application areas of artificial intelligence, and to equip them with the ability to use AI methods such as problem solving, learning, decision making, and algorithm development in the context of management information systems.
courses.description: The course begins with an introduction to the definition, history, and basic concepts of artificial intelligence. It examines the relationship between AI and management information systems, with particular emphasis on its contribution to decision-making processes. Core techniques such as search algorithms (breadth-first, depth-first, heuristic search) and knowledge representation methods (propositional and predicate logic) are studied. Expert systems and decision support systems are explained through examples of how AI is used in business settings. The course also introduces machine learning, including supervised and unsupervised learning methods. Fundamental algorithms such as decision trees, k-nearest neighbors (k-NN), and naive Bayes, as well as the basics of artificial neural networks, are covered. Natural language processing (NLP) technologies are introduced, and current applications such as chatbots and recommendation systems are examined. In the later parts of the course, the ethical aspects of AI, bias, transparency in decision-making, and social impacts are discussed. Finally, students are expected to develop a small-scale AI application, reinforcing their theoretical knowledge through practice.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete this course will understand the fundamental concepts, history, and approaches of artificial intelligence and will be able to evaluate this knowledge in the context of management information systems.
They can identify and apply basic AI methods such as search algorithms, knowledge representation, and logic to appropriate problems.
They will understand the structure of expert systems and decision support systems and explain how these systems can be used in business processes.
Students will also learn the basic principles of machine learning and analyze algorithms such as decision trees, k-nearest neighbors, and naive Bayes.
They will grasp the basic principles of artificial neural networks and natural language processing techniques and explain their practical use cases.
By developing awareness of the ethical, social, and legal impacts of AI, they will evaluate AI applications with a sense of responsibility.
Finally, they will design and develop simple AI-based solutions for real-world problems, strengthening their applied skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the definition, history, and basic concepts of artificial intelligence
Hafta 2: The relationship between artificial intelligence and management information systems
Hafta 3: Introduction to search algorithms: breadth-first and depth-first search
Hafta 4: Heuristic search methods and applications
Hafta 5: Knowledge representation: propositional logic and predicate logic
Hafta 6: Expert systems and decision support systems
Hafta 7: The contribution of artificial intelligence to decision-making processes
Hafta 9: Introduction to machine learning: supervised and unsupervised learning
Hafta 10: Decision trees, k-nearest neighbors (k-NN), naive Bayes algorithms
Hafta 11: Fundamentals of artificial neural networks
Hafta 12: Introduction to natural language processing (NLP) and chatbots
Hafta 13: Recommendation systems and other current AI applications
Hafta 14: Ethics, bias, and transparency in artificial intelligence
Hafta 15: Social and legal impacts of AI + project preparation

courses.name: Branding and Brand Supports
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamental concepts, strategies, and applications of branding. It also seeks to develop the analytical and decision-making skills needed for successful brand management by covering topics such as brand identity, brand positioning, brand equity, brand communication, and consumer perception. In addition, students are expected to understand the brand creation process through national and international examples.
courses.description: Branding and the marketing mix, creating strong brands, marketing and communication programs, measuring brand performance, brand auditing, brand design and structures, and managing brand equity for long-term success.
learning_outcomes: Learn relevant concepts
Understand product and brand management concepts
Comprehend strategies such as brand awareness and positioning in the global market environment
Understand sustainable brand management in a market environment where cultural differences are considered
Understand changing trends and technologies to improve brand management skills
Understand branding and relate concepts through case studies
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Definition of the brand, positioning, market segmentation, consumer behavior
Hafta 4: Brand loyalty, perceived quality, brand identity
Hafta 6: Brand equity and brand extension

courses.name: Extended Reality Technologies with XR Turkey
courses.purpose: This course aims to comprehensively examine the fundamental principles, application areas, and social impacts of extended reality (XR) technologies such as augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and mixed reality (MR). In collaboration with XR Turkey, students will gain both theoretical knowledge and experience through current examples of sectoral practices. By the end of the course, students are expected to be able to creatively analyze and evaluate XR technologies and develop their own projects.
courses.description: This course covers the theoretical foundations and practical applications of augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and mixed reality (MR) technologies in depth. It addresses the historical development of XR technologies, technical components, user experience design, and narrative structures, while focusing on usage examples in fields such as education, health, architecture, and culture. Within the course, students have the opportunity to listen to invited speakers from the sector and participate in workshops in collaboration with XR Turkey. Students are expected to develop a creative and technically strong XR project at the end of the term.
learning_outcomes: The student can explain the basic concepts and historical development of XR technologies.
The student can analyze XR applications and evaluate usage examples in different fields.
The student can develop creative and technical skills to design and present XR content at a basic level.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: What is XR? Concepts and Scope
Hafta 2: History and Development of XR Technologies
Hafta 3: Augmented Reality (AR): Basic Technologies and Areas of Use
Hafta 4: Virtual Reality (VR): Hardware, Software, and Experience Design
Hafta 5: Mixed Reality (MR) and the Extended Reality Spectrum
Hafta 6: XR and Storytelling: Narrative Structures and Interaction
Hafta 7: Design with XR: Experiential Design and User-Centeredness

Hafta 10: XR in Education: Redesigning Learning Environments
Hafta 11: XR Applications in Health and Architecture

courses.name: CapitArt-X Media Art Lab
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to help students develop an interdisciplinary understanding in the fields of digital art, artificial intelligence, and media aesthetics. Students will enhance their skills in creative production, curatorial thinking, and critical media practice so that they can actively participate in the contemporary digital art environment.
courses.description: This course addresses digital art, artificial intelligence, and media aesthetics through an interdisciplinary perspective that combines theoretical foundations with creative production. Students begin by examining the historical development and basic concepts of digital art, then explore the effects of artificial intelligence on artistic production and aesthetics. Throughout the course, students carry out practical work involving curatorial thinking, creative coding, data visualization, and interactive media applications, while also discussing the ethical and social dimensions of art and technology. Within midterm and final projects, students are given the opportunity to produce and exhibit original digital artworks. The course also focuses on virtual and augmented reality, exhibition design strategies, and the integration of art and technology, thereby developing both the critical and creative capacities of students.
learning_outcomes: Students will gain theoretical and practical knowledge in digital art and artificial intelligence.
Students will develop curatorial thinking and exhibition design skills.
Students will take an active role in the field of digital art by developing creative projects.

Hafta 3: Media Aesthetics and Visual Culture

Hafta 8: Data Visualization and Aesthetic Interfaces
Hafta 9: Virtual and Augmented Reality in Art
Hafta 10: Ethics and Society in Digital Art
Hafta 12: Curatorial Strategy and Exhibition Planning

courses.name: Women in Games & We are in Games!
courses.purpose: This course critically examines the roles, representations, and participation of women and gender-diverse individuals in the game industry. Students explore structural inequalities in the sector, analyze character representations in games, learn inclusive design practices, and evaluate professional development pathways for women and marginalized communities.
courses.description: This course addresses gender representation, equality, and inclusivity in the game industry from an interdisciplinary perspective. Students examine the historical and contemporary roles of women and gender-diverse individuals in games, critically evaluate narrative and visual stereotypes in character design, and study inclusive game development practices, accessibility, labor conditions, workplace discrimination, and the contributions of women in both mainstream and independent game spaces.
learning_outcomes: Critically analyze gender representations in video games, including character design, narratives, and player roles.
Identify structural barriers faced by women and gender-diverse individuals in the game industry and propose inclusive strategies.
Develop and present an original project, creative or analytical, that reflects a gender-focused perspective on game culture or design.

Hafta 9: Queer and feminist independent games

Gender and Media
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine the role of media in constructing gender and its intersections with race, ethnicity, class, and sexuality. It also addresses the importance of diversity in media industries and the role of new media technologies in challenging and/or reaffirming traditional gender structures.
courses.description: This course examines representations of race, class, gender, and sexual identity in the media. Students discuss authorship, audiences, and how various media contents such as film, television, print journalism, and advertising enable, facilitate, and challenge social structures in society. The course explores how gender and race affect media production and discusses the impact of new media and digital media through contemporary media users. Students analyze sexist and racialized language and regulation as produced online in blogs, vlogs, avatars, and the construction of cyber identities. The course provides an introduction to feminist approaches to media studies, drawing on work in feminist film theory, journalism, cultural studies, gender and politics, and cyberfeminism.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes diversity among audiences, content, and media producers.
Identifies stereotypes of gender, race, class, and sexual identity in media representations.
Finds examples of framing, intersectionality, and symbolic annihilation in the media.
Analyzes texts in the context of cultural and social identities by considering how reality is socially and discursively constructed by the media.
Discusses media literacy in contemporary terms in light of 21st-century developments in online cultural production and new media.
Understands the basic theories and methods of examining media, power, and social identities.

Hafta 2: Gender and Media: From Gender Roles to Social Construction and Beyond

courses.name: Business and Society
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students gain knowledge about business and the social environment of business, including factors such as social responsibility, ethics, ecology, and sustainable growth. It also aims to enable students to learn current topics and concepts in the course so that they can make quick and accurate decisions and succeed in the businesses where they will work.
courses.description: Introduction to the course and basic concepts, examination of the business model, examination of the hierarchy of objectives, the impact of globalization on businesses, technology as an environmental factor, the concept of social responsibility in businesses and socially responsible management approach, the concept of ethics in business and related dilemmas, image in businesses, sustainable development in business, shareholders as an environmental factor and corporate governance, media as an environmental factor, ecological approaches in business, reputation in business, and business in the 21st century.
learning_outcomes: To gain knowledge about business and society
To learn the stakeholder approach
To learn the connection between corporate governance and strategic management
To learn external and internal environmental factors
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course and basic concepts
Hafta 2: Examination of the business model
Hafta 3: Examination of the hierarchy of objectives
Hafta 6: The concept of social responsibility in businesses and socially responsible management approach
Hafta 7: The concept of ethics in business and related dilemmas
Hafta 10: The concept of sustainability in business
Hafta 11: Shareholders as an environmental factor and corporate governance
resources: Ebert and Griffin. Business Essentials 12e, Prentice Hall, 2017
Goss, D. (2015). Small Business and Society (Routledge Revivals). Routledge.

courses.name: Introduction to New Media
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop critical conceptual frameworks for understanding how new media technologies are used to make communication processes more effective in various areas of daily life.
courses.description: This course offers a comprehensive approach to rapidly developing new media technologies. It conceptualizes new media in the context of new forms of participatory culture, sources of information and entertainment, platforms for social participation, artistic media tools, and governmental tools, and examines the impact of new media technology on transforming culture and society. Topics include social media, digital games, digital media art, digital and citizen journalism, the digital transformation of television and cinema, and social control in the digital age.
learning_outcomes: Define the defining characteristics of new media.
Understand and interpret the new media environment.
Investigate the economic, social, political, and cultural effects of new media technology.
Identify and explore areas where new media technology can be applied to improve communication.
Generate innovative ideas to develop, integrate, and apply new media technology aimed at enriching culture and society.
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: New media as a source of information
Hafta 6: New media as a source of information
Hafta 10: New media as a tool of artistic expression
Hafta 11: New media as a tool of artistic expression
Hafta 12: New media as a form of governance
resources: Excerpts of selected articles and books.
Lecture notes

courses.name: Introduction to Sociology
courses.purpose: This course teaches students the basic elements of the discipline of sociology. By the end of the term, students will have foundational knowledge about the sources of social conflict and cooperation. They will also develop critical and analytical thinking skills for examining society and human interactions.
courses.description: Sociology can be defined as the systematic study of human society and social life. In this course, students will explore questions such as how human behavior is shaped, what the foundations of social conflict and cooperation are, what social processes affect people's ways of thinking, feelings, structures, and behaviors, and how people shape the social pressures they encounter. The course also introduces basic sociological theories, including structural functionalism, social conflict, symbolic interactionism, and feminism.
learning_outcomes: Learn sociological theories
Learn critical sociological thinking
weekly_topics: Hafta 14: Collective Behavior and Social Movements
resources: Heather Griffiths, et al. Introduction to Sociology, 2nd Edition, OpenStax Rice University, 2017 (open access book, no purchase required): https://openstax.org/details/introduction-sociology-2e

courses.name: Introduction to International Relations
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to help students understand what international relations consist of, how they are shaped, and the elements that form these factors.
courses.description: The course examines the main actors, issues, and theories of international relations.
learning_outcomes: Understand the foundations of theories of international relations.
Learn the events that affect and shape international politics.
Learn the internal and external factors affecting the decision-making processes of state and non-state actors.
Gain the ability to approach major events in a globalizing world analytically.
Learn the concepts of power and balance of power and analyze them in different dimensions.
Recognize current developments and problems occurring on a global scale.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: What is International Relations?: Concept and Development of the Discipline
Hafta 4: Theories of International Relations: Realism & Liberalism
Hafta 5: Theories of International Relations: Feminist and Critical Approaches
Hafta 10: Other Actors: NGOs; Multinational Companies
resources: Devetak, R.; Burke, A.; George, J. (2012) (2nd Ed.) An Introduction to International Relations, Cambridge: Cambridge Uni. Press.
Course Textbook

courses.name: Introduction to Modern Philosophy
courses.purpose: To learn the evolution of philosophy from the Enlightenment to the present through important works, concepts, and philosophical movements, and to understand its effects on literary works and critical methods.
courses.description: Historical philosophical movements, important socio-philosophical works and concepts of the 19th and 20th centuries, metaphysics, modern philosophical views, modern philosophers, ethics, politics, time and identity, and justice.
learning_outcomes: Define the philosophical and social ideas that led to various philosophical and social movements such as realism, idealism, Marxism, liberalism, modernism, and postmodernism.
Define important socio-philosophical works and conceptualizations of the 19th and 20th centuries.
Evaluate and use psychoanalysis in all its aspects.
Describe the reciprocal exchange relationship between socio-philosophical thought and literature and their mutual influences.
Define the factors that led to movements such as modernism and post-modernism and their relationship with literary and artistic production and consumption.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General Introduction to the course; What is modern philosophy?; Epistemology on stage
Hafta 13: Philosophy of Language and Hermeneutics
resources: Durant, Will. The Story of Philosophy: The Lives and Opinions of the Greater Philosophers. Simon and Schuster, 1961.
Palmer, Donald. Looking at Philosophy: The Unbearable Heaviness of Philosophy Made Lighter. 4th ed. McGraw-Hill, 2005.

courses.name: Sociology of Inequality
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of social inequality by introducing key sociological concepts and theories related to inequality and social stratification. It also encourages students to critically analyze different forms of inequality, recognize their intersections, and evaluate strategies for addressing and reducing inequality in various contexts.
courses.description: The course covers a broad range of topics including theoretical perspectives, social stratification, economic inequality, race and ethnicity, gender issues, and intersectionality. It also examines how inequality manifests in education, health, the environment, and on a global scale, and discusses the role of media, technology, and the Sustainable Development Goals in addressing social inequalities.
learning_outcomes: Understand and apply key theoretical perspectives on social inequality.
Critically analyze different forms of inequality, including economic, racial, gender, and global inequalities.
Recognize the intersectionality of various forms of inequality.
Evaluate the role of institutions such as education, healthcare, and media in sustaining or reducing inequality.
Discuss the impact of inequality on individuals, communities, and societies.
Evaluate strategies and policies aimed at reducing social inequality, including the Sustainable Development Goals.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the Sociology of Inequality
Hafta 14: Sustainable Development Goals and Their Impact on Reducing Social Inequality

courses.name: Approaches to English Language Teaching
courses.purpose: This course examines approaches to foreign language teaching from the past to the present, including the grammar-translation method and contemporary communicative methods. It emphasizes how formal and natural foreign language learning have developed and how an effective foreign language teaching method can be developed.
courses.description: Definition of language learning, general learning theories, language learning theories, neurolinguistics, sociolinguistics, and psycholinguistics.
learning_outcomes: Define language learning
Describe general learning approaches
Describe general language learning approaches
Express the connection between language and the brain
Evaluate language learning from a psycholinguistic perspective

courses.name: Linguistic Approach to Translation
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the theoretical foundations and practical studies of translation from English into Turkish from a linguistic perspective. It also provides opportunities to discuss textual and dynamic equivalence and aims to develop students' text analysis skills based on subject-predicate theory.
courses.description: Introduction to the theoretical foundations and practical studies of translation from English into Turkish from a linguistic perspective.
learning_outcomes: The student integrates theoretical knowledge with translation practice by applying the approaches adopted in translation to texts.
The student discusses, interprets, and makes suggestions regarding translation studies in terms of approaches and theory.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: concepts of translation and linguistics
Hafta 2: The purpose and scope of translation studies in general
Hafta 11: Subject-predicate applications at sentence level
Hafta 12: Subject-predicate applications at text level
Hafta 13: Subject-predicate and rhetorical features of a text

courses.name: Research Methods in English Literature
courses.purpose: This course provides students with knowledge about the definition of research, research methods, research questions, hypothesis formation, data collection and data analysis, evaluating, interpreting and reporting data, sample theses, literature review methods, and conducting sample research.
courses.description: The course covers the definition of research, research methods, research questions, hypothesis formation, data collection and data analysis, evaluating, interpreting and reporting data, sample theses, literature review methods, and conducting sample research.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the main research tools, techniques, and approaches.
Select the best tools, techniques, and approaches to conduct effective research and obtain efficient results.
Organize research according to stages, phases, and steps to carry it out more efficiently.
Become familiar with the basic elements of academic research reports and produce clear, well-organized, and in-depth analytical reports of their research.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: How do we know what we know?
Hafta 6: Annotated Bibliography: What and How
Hafta 7: MIDTERM ASSIGNMENT (Research question and annotated bibliography due)
Hafta 10: Literature Reviews: How to and Why

courses.name: Teaching English Language Skills
courses.purpose: The course is designed to equip students with theoretical knowledge and practical skills about language skills, the ability to teach reading, listening, speaking, and writing for various educational levels, and the competence to select, grade, and develop instructional materials for teaching language skills.
courses.description: Foreign language skills, foreign language sub-skills, approaches to teaching language skills, methods of teaching language skills, and preparing lesson plans for language teaching.
learning_outcomes: To be able to teach language skills aimed at meeting daily needs
To be able to teach sub-skills related to language skills methodologically
To develop practice activities for language skills
To be able to use the skill teaching method by following the preparation, development, and personalization stages

Hafta 9: Educational technologies and other learning resources, issues in language learning
Hafta 10: Educational technologies and other learning resources, grammar teaching

courses.name: Human Rights Mechanisms
courses.purpose: The course aims to introduce students to what human rights encompass and how they are implemented.
courses.description: Basic human rights philosophy, principles, instruments and institutions; an overview of current issues and debates in the field; the dimensions of the complex structures of international human rights law with both national and international applications; major developments and documents in this area; and mechanisms with special focus on the decisions of regional and global organizations regarding the ECHR and Turkey.
learning_outcomes: Understand the philosophical foundations of the concept of human rights.
Know the historical development of the concept of human rights.
Become familiar with opposing views in human rights debates.
Discuss different applications in terms of time and place.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Philosophical Foundations of the Concept of Human Rights
Hafta 5: Punishment of Human Rights Violations
Hafta 6: Review of the Universal Human Rights Convention
Hafta 7: Foreign Policy and Human Rights
Hafta 11: European Convention on Human Rights

courses.name: Global Business Trends
courses.purpose: To understand the effects of globalization and develop strategic thinking skills about global business issues. It equips students with the basic facts of globalization, its economic foundations, and the key trends and institutions shaping the global economy and the future of international business management. It also helps students understand macroeconomic concepts, the role of government policies in determining macroeconomic outcomes, and the effects of the economic environment on global and international trade.
courses.description: Historical and contemporary global business trends, the history of business, industrial revolutions, Industry 4.0, digitalization, and new business models and trends in the new century.
learning_outcomes: To gain knowledge about the processes the business world has gone through from past to present
To learn the changes created by industrial revolutions in the business world
To learn the impact of Industry 4.0 and digitalization on global business trends
To have an understanding of global business trends in light of political, economic, and technological indicators
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Scope of the course and an overview of the global economy
Hafta 2: Overview of the business world
Hafta 3: History of the Industrial Revolution
resources: Lecturer's notes

courses.name: Art and Culture
courses.purpose: The course aims to provide students with a basic understanding of how the world of art and culture functions. Through visual materials and critical texts, it introduces art as a modern category and emphasizes the role of art in contemporary society within the context of other cultural productions.
courses.description: This is a weekly elective course open to students from all departments. It introduces the basic contexts, concepts, and institutional structures needed to understand artistic and cultural productions. The course examines how artistic and cultural products are produced, interpreted, and presented to audiences, with special emphasis on museums as key institutions that distinguish, preserve, historicize, and assign value to these productions.
learning_outcomes: Students understand basic concepts and ideas related to art and culture.
Students comprehend the institutional structures behind artistic and cultural productions.
Students gain the ability to evaluate and interpret creative productions and cultural institutions within social, historical, and cultural contexts.
Students develop research, critical thinking, speaking, and writing skills through presentations and research assignments on topics related to art and cultural productions.

Hafta 2: The Changing Meaning of Art
Hafta 3: The Artist, the Artwork, and the Audience
Hafta 4: Cultural Heritage: Destruction and Protection

courses.name: Basic Knowledge in Gastronomy
courses.purpose: It is aimed to ensure that students learn the basic concepts of gastronomy applications and to provide a certain infrastructure for the courses they will take in other semesters.
courses.description: Gastronomy is an art that includes tasting, discovering, researching, experiencing, writing, and understanding the sensory characteristics of food preparation and human nutrition. It also includes the study of nutrition with various cultures. The application of chemical and biological knowledge in cooking has begun to be known as molecular gastronomy.

courses.name: Digital Communication and Culture
courses.purpose: To define and analyze digital culture through relevant theoretical perspectives in cultural studies, to critically examine the relationship between new media technologies and cultural practices, to engage in scholarly discussions about cultural forms shaped by digital and networked communication, and to examine our roles as consumers and cultural producers in a data-rich, digitized, and networked society.
courses.description: This course investigates contemporary cultural phenomena shaped by digital communication technologies. Drawing on digital humanities, memory studies, audience studies, game studies, material culture, and posthuman studies, it analyzes the formation of public memory, community, subculture, identity, subjectivity, and agency.
learning_outcomes: Defines the characteristics of contemporary digital culture.
Applies theoretical perspectives from cultural studies to analyze digital cultural phenomena.
Understands the role of digital media in shaping community, subculture, identity, subjectivity, and agency.
Formulates critical views on current issues in digital culture.
Critically reflects on everyday interactions with digital media and on roles as media professionals.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Internet, networked communication, and computer culture
Hafta 4: Cultural data and cultural analysis
Hafta 14: Culture in the posthuman and digital posthuman age
resources: Lecture notes

courses.name: Media and Society
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain the connection between media and society in the context of media theories.
courses.description: This course will cover the development, effects, and functions of mass media.
learning_outcomes: Understand mass media and mass communication
Understand the effects of media on society
Understand media theories
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Basic concepts in mass communication and mass media

courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid foundation for understanding what computer games are about, how games can be used in teaching and learning, and the discussions on how games affect players.
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to basic concepts and definition of serious gaming. The following topics are covered: gamification, gamification and behavior, improving behavior through serious games and gamification, developing serious game design document, integrating learning theories into serious games.
learning_outcomes: Learn the definition of serious games and how they differ from gamification
Learn the basic definitions, concepts, and theories surrounding gamification
Learn how behavior can be affected by gamification
Learn how to develop a serious game design
Learn how learning theories can be applied to serious game design

courses.name: Healthy Life and Physical Activity
courses.purpose: To introduce the concepts of health and physical activity and to evaluate the importance of activity and exercise in a healthy life.
courses.description: This course covers the definition of health and basic health information, physical activity, exercise and sport, physical fitness, principles of exercise training, different types of exercise, exercise prescription for different age groups, indications and contraindications of exercise, nutrition, and the effects of technology on health.
learning_outcomes: Explains exercise techniques.
Selects appropriate exercise methods for different populations.
Has knowledge about yoga, pilates, body awareness, and stabilization.
Has knowledge about healthy nutrition.
Relates exercise to technology and designs exercise programs in an innovative way.

courses.name: Film Culture
courses.purpose: This course aims to focus on the fundamental developments in film and film technology; the dramatic aspects of film; cinematography, film aesthetics, genres, auteur studies, and the sociological consequences of film are among its main objectives.
courses.description: This course provides an introductory examination of film as a distinct art form capable of reflecting, criticizing, reshaping, and influencing society and culture. The course discusses film narrative formulation and the main formal elements of film: mise-en-scène, cinematography, editing, and sound design. In addition, terminology for critically examining and analyzing film through various genres in relation to different aspects of society and culture, such as violence, race, class, gender, and sexuality, is also discussed.
learning_outcomes: Classifies the necessary information related to film aesthetics.
Gains an understanding of various films as products of specific cultures, times, and places.
Understands critical interpretations related to film from a multidisciplinary and interdisciplinary perspective.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course: discussing basic cinema and film terms
Hafta 2: Basic concepts about cinema and film
Hafta 4: Film styles and film genres
Hafta 10: National cinema: Soviet montage cinema
Hafta 12: National cinema: New German cinema
Hafta 14: Cultural, social, and cultural effects of film

courses.name: Visual Thinking
courses.purpose: The course provides students with a basic understanding of the principles of visual culture. It also aims to explore the artistic and theoretical dimensions of visual culture and enables students to acquire the critical methods needed to evaluate cinematography and visual expression aesthetically.
courses.description: This course is about how images are constructed to work with and against the way we see. It is designed especially for those who want to share their personal artistic vision with others through still or moving image creation, but it may also benefit anyone interested in expanding creativity, especially poets, writers, visual artists, and theatre artists.
learning_outcomes: Examine and interpret artworks and writings in the field of visual arts
Communicate ideas visually through photographs and moving images
Discuss and defend their work orally and provide constructive feedback to others in group critiques

courses.name: Building Managerial Skills
courses.purpose: The course aims to equip participants with the knowledge and skills of leading managerial positions within the framework of modern management approaches. It also teaches the skills and practices needed to take successful steps in change processes and focuses on employee-manager relations in the changing business world.
courses.description: This course covers basic management concepts, management by objectives, development of managerial personality, development of managerial skills through decision making and problem solving, team building and management, leadership and motivation, time management, and conflict management.
learning_outcomes: Explains the relationship between managers and related concepts.
Understands and relates the evolution process of management and leadership theories.
Interprets the effects of the manager inside and outside the organization.
Understands the manager's decision-making process and employee relations.
Explains the changing roles of the manager.
Understands the causes of conflicts in the workplace and knows conflict management strategies.
Can apply time management principles.

courses.name: Introduction to Business Functions
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to fundamental business concepts and practices, inform them about the business environment, business ethics and corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, and the global business context, and provide an understanding of management, organization, motivation, leadership, and marketing.
courses.description: This course is designed to introduce students to the business world and show how businesses operate in a changing business environment. The course provides students with knowledge about the business environment, business ethics and corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, and the global business context, and provides a foundation in management, organization, motivation, leadership, and marketing.
learning_outcomes: Define basic business concepts and practices.
Explain the business environment, business ethics and corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, and the global business context.
Outline the concepts and practices in business management and organization.
Analyze issues in management, organization, motivation, leadership, and marketing.

courses.name: International Business Environment
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to environmental forces in international trade, environmental forecasting, and environmental analysis; explain major issues in the international competitive, economic, and technological environments; familiarize students with the international cultural, demographic, and social environment; provide an understanding of ecological environmental issues; inform students about international political and legal environmental issues; and enable students to research, prepare, and present a project as a team on the course topics.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental areas of the international business environment. Developments and innovations in transportation, production, communication, and technology are leading to a truly global economy. As the global marketplace develops, the need for businesspeople to understand the subtle differences of doing business overseas and across borders increases. Designed for future managers who will engage in and be affected by international ventures, the course addresses the key dimensions of the international business environment.
learning_outcomes: Define basic business concepts and practices related to environmental forces, environmental forecasting, and environmental analysis in international trade.
Analyze the forces that create competition in the international business environment.
Explain the major issues in international economic and technological environments.
Evaluate the elements of the international cultural, demographic, and social environment.
Identify the major issues in international ecological, political, and legal environments.
Research, prepare, and present a project on the course topics as a team member.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Environmental forces in international trade
Hafta 3: Environmental forecasting and environmental analysis in international trade
Hafta 8: International cultural, demographic, and social environment
Hafta 12: Review of course and final exam topics

Worthington, I., Britton, C., & Thompson, E. (2019). The Business Environment: A Global Perspective (8th ed.). Pearson.

courses.name: Design Thinking
courses.purpose: This course introduces User-Centered Design and Design Thinking through some basic principles of interaction design for products and services. It focuses on human needs, preferences, and experiences rather than technological factors, and provides a framework for understanding and applying empathy, user connection, problem framing, idea generation, design, testing, prototyping, role-playing, and touch-points.
courses.description: The course provides students with theoretical, conceptual, and applied knowledge and practices that may be useful when designing products, services, interaction systems, and systems within the scope of Design Thinking and human-centered design.
learning_outcomes: Explain the principles and examples of empathic design and instinctive design.
Apply the basic methods of User-Centered Design to define personas and target audiences.
Create an innovative and original project within the framework of the three basic criteria of User-Centered Design: feasible technology, viable business plan, and empathic design.

Hafta 2: What is a human-centered approach?

courses.name: Basic Issues in Sociology
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop and enrich students’ sociological imagination. It also motivates students to think more critically about social issues and to analyze social life critically.
courses.description: This course explores basic questions in sociology based on current research, literature, and foundational sociological theories. It examines the links between social structures/institutions, culture, and human experiences. Students learn to follow major debates around core sociological questions and evaluate popular discourse from a critical sociological perspective.

courses.name: History of Economics
courses.purpose: The course aims to provide students with a basic understanding of the historical development of economic processes and institutions, changes in production, patterns of distribution and consumption, and the general evolution of production factors in the world, especially in Western Europe.
courses.description: The course begins with some concepts and definitions related to economics and economic history. It continues with the economic understandings of ancient civilizations, the Middle Ages, and the pre-capitalist world. It analyzes the emergence of the modern economy at the dawn of the Industrial Revolution. With the Industrial Revolution, modern economy and its many new institutions are introduced, and modern civilizations and the world economy up to the 21st century are covered.
learning_outcomes: Students gain a general understanding of the history of the world economy.
Students learn the differences between leading and follower economies in world economic history.
Students understand the reasons for the successes and failures of leading and follower nations in history.
Students learn the effects of government policies on markets and economic actors.

courses.name: Economics of Technology & Innovation
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students’ analytical understanding of the economics of technology and innovation. It examines how innovation drives economic growth, reshapes competition, and transforms industries and institutions. It also helps students understand the roles of firms, financial systems, institutions, and public policies in supporting technological change in modern economies.
courses.description: This course examines innovation as a structural force that drives long-run economic growth and reshapes capitalist economies. It addresses the limits of capital accumulation and explains productivity dynamics through Solow and Schumpeterian frameworks. The course analyzes firm behavior, competition, institutions, and national innovation systems to understand how innovation emerges, spreads, and transforms economic structures. It also covers historical technological revolutions, catching-up processes, and structural transformation. The course concludes with discussions on inequality, finance, competition policy, and the sustainability of innovation-driven capitalism.
learning_outcomes: Explain the relationship between innovation, productivity dynamics, and long-run economic growth.
Analyze how firms compete through innovation in dynamic and uncertain markets.
Evaluate the role of institutions, financial systems, and public policies in shaping innovation systems.
Compare countries’ development paths in terms of technological capability, absorptive capacity, and structural transformation.

Apply key theoretical frameworks, including Solow, Schumpeterian, and evolutionary approaches, to interpret technological change and its broader economic consequences, including inequality and sustainability.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the Course; Course Syllabus
Hafta 2: Innovation and the Long-Run Growth Puzzle
Hafta 4: The Evolutionary Theory of the Firm
Hafta 9: Technological Revolutions and Long Waves
Hafta 10: Catch-up Growth and Structural Transformation

courses.name: Freewill
courses.purpose: This course aims to explore, explain, and discuss various philosophical arguments concerning the concept of free will in historical and thematic terms.
courses.description: This course provides students with an introductory understanding of the fundamental philosophical questions related to the concept of free will. In line with the need for conceptual analysis in philosophical inquiry, the course begins with an introduction focused on the concepts of will, free will, and determinism. It then examines the approaches of ten thinkers from Ancient Greece to the twentieth century under four modules, following a historical sequence. Based on the discussions throughout the term, the course also addresses important philosophical issues closely connected to free will, such as individual responsibility, the problem of evil, the possibility of morality, philosophical freedom, and the foundations of law and justice.
learning_outcomes: Students will learn to read philosophical texts actively.
Students will gain knowledge of debates on some important philosophical questions throughout the history of philosophy.
Students will learn to reason creatively.
Students will learn to think critically about fundamental philosophical problems.
Students will develop a historical perspective on different areas of philosophy.

courses.name: Introduction to Political Sociology
courses.purpose: This course focuses on the network of relations among the state, the individual, and society within the framework of the basic concepts and theoretical approaches of political sociology and related contemporary debates. It aims to help students make sense of social and political formations occurring in the contemporary world through major theories of political sociology.
courses.description: The course covers elites, social classes, the state, political parties and processes, media, social movements and related organizations, as well as the structures shaping modern societies such as market, state, and civil society. It also addresses power, domination, legitimacy, the state, elites and their social networks, social class and inequality, protest, collective action, and social movements.
learning_outcomes: Has the ability to understand theories and theoretical perspectives in sociology and the social sciences.
Has the knowledge to use basic theoretical analyses and sociological concepts in their own work.
Has the knowledge and skills to interpret social, economic, and historical phenomena sociologically by reasoning within a social, economic, and historical framework.
Has the ability to follow and evaluate classical and contemporary Turkish and English studies and research in the world in their field.
Uses the theoretical framework and research methods appropriate for sociological research in a future project.
Relates research questions to the findings obtained from the research and reports the results.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course of Political Sociology, beginning with two historically significant striking examples: Magna Carta and the Rosa Parks event.
Hafta 2: The pioneering place of Thorstein Veblen (1857-1929), praised by the German social scientist Max Gustav Lange, in Political Sociology.
Hafta 3: Political Sociology as an interdisciplinary subfield of sociology, shaped since the early 1930s in the context of developments such as communism, fascism, and the Second World War.
Hafta 4: Other relevant thinkers: Machiavelli, Tocqueville, M. Weber, K. Marx, C.W. Mills. Two prominent Turkish social scientists: Niyazi Berkes and Behice Boran.

Hafta 5: A comprehensive analysis of the theme of power, covering all relevant dimensions: definition, related analogies, types of power, related networks, institutionalized power in a society, visibility and power of women, and the ancient Turkish concept of "kut" as a sacred gift for governing.
Hafta 6: Institutions that especially show the visibility and implementation of power and authority, such as the army, police, and prison administration.

Hafta 7: The situation in which power on the international level enables a stronger state to influence a weaker one in some way: the example of the return of Turkic new states to the Russian Cyrillic alphabet after a long interval; Japanese, Prussian, American, and Turkish military traditions and historical lines.
resources: Dobratz, Betty A. et al. (2019). Power, Politics & Society: An Introduction to Political Sociology, Routledge, New York & London.
Janoski, Thomas et al. (Eds.) (2005). The Handbook of Political Sociology: States, Civil Societies & Globalization, Cambridge University Press, New York & Melbourne.

courses.name: Introduction to English Literature
courses.purpose: This course examines specific thematic structures and critical perspectives encountered in literary studies through the concept of genre. It focuses on fictional texts, especially the short story, and analyzes its structure and basic elements.

Understand the critical frameworks and categories related to short story studies.
Master the critical language used in discussions of fictional texts.
Gain the ability to interpret sample texts from different historical periods.
Write an assignment on a literary text using a documentation style.

courses.name: Gender and Media
courses.purpose: This course aims to examine the role of media in constructing representations related to gender, as well as race, class, ethnicity, and sexuality. It also investigates the central role played by media industries in the production and reproduction of gender roles across different media.
courses.description: This course focuses on basic concepts and approaches related to gender and the relationship between gender and media. It highlights the importance of media industries in the production of ideology and meaning, and questions the role of media industries in the production and reproduction of gender roles. While basic approaches to gender are discussed in their historical context, gender representations in cultural products produced by media and cultural industries are examined.
learning_outcomes: Distinguishes between sex and gender concepts.
Understands the role of the media industry in the production and reproduction of ideology and gender.
Gains knowledge of basic approaches in media and communication studies related to media industry, ideology production, audience, and reception studies.
Analyzes gender discrimination in content produced across different media channels.
Identifies gender, race, class, and sexual identity stereotypes in cultural products produced by the media industry.

courses.name: Introduction to Metaverse
courses.purpose: The main objective of the course is to develop within students an understanding of the core components of the metaverse and an awareness of its potential impacts on society. By the end of the class, students will possess developed ethical positions on many of the important metaverse topics.
courses.description: The course introduces fundamental elements that form the foundation of various conceptualizations of “The Metaverse”. Topics to be presented and discussed include shared spatialization, digital mediation of reality, socialization, and assigning value to digital objects. The course will devote significant time to discussions of ethics and the impacts that digitization will have on non-digital aspects of society. Students will be required to research and write several essays throughout the course and design a metaverse scenario as a final group project. Online class sessions will frequently take place inside of 2D and 3D “metaverse platforms”. Students will be expected to know how to use their keyboard and mouse/touchpad to navigate these spaces and to use their microphone effectively. This is not a course focused on cryptographic topics. Blockchains, cryptocurrencies, and NFTs will not be the focus of the course, although these subjects will be included in discussions of metaverse economics and concepts of ownership.

Metaverse'de eşzamanlı öğrenmeye yönelik araçları ve yöntemleri keşfeder
Kapsayıcı Metaverse tasarımında erişilebilirlik ve eşitlik hususlarını bilir
Metaverse öğrenmesinde farklı bakış açılarının ve kültürlerin etkisini analiz eder

courses.name: Unhealthy Brilliants

The course will discuss hepatitis B/cirrhosis and composer Beethoven's life.

The course will discuss Robert Downey Jr. and alcoholism/substance use disorder.
The course will discuss burnout syndrome and Eric Yuan.
The course will discuss acne and Emma Stone.

The course will discuss atheromatous disease and Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen Abdul Kalam (cardiac arrest).
The course will discuss chronic kidney disease and Aries Merritt.
The course will discuss autoimmune diseases and Renoir (rheumatoid arthritis).
The course will discuss Alzheimer disease and Gabriel García Márquez (lymphoma and AD).

courses.name: Digital Media and Social Media Management
courses.purpose: The general aim of this course is to examine and learn all stages, characteristics, dynamics, elements, and application methods of the transfer of the marketing concept to cyber environments as a result of developing technologies and communication opportunities. Among the general objectives of the course are to examine and learn digital marketing models, advertising and public relations applications, new concepts and evolving marketing strategies, digital marketing campaigns, brand positioning in digital environments, e-commerce, and mobile marketing applications.
courses.description: This course covers search engine advertising, search engine optimization, the web concept, social media, and changing e-commerce trends in digital environments. It presents data and applications related to the digitalization of the 4P approach, which is defined as the marketing mix. In addition, digital brand and digital marketing trends are discussed in a world where social media has transformed from content into an e-commerce environment.
learning_outcomes: To examine concepts related to cyberspace and have knowledge of evaluating processes within their historical development
To learn the characteristics of digital marketing strategies and applications
To examine digital campaign applications and gain the competence to apply them actively
To follow the developments in digital marketing dynamics, design future scenarios, and evaluate the digital world with a marketing perspective in the context of commerce
To use digital marketing tools in their basic aspects and analyze performance and campaign metrics by reading them
To become familiar with the concept of S-Commerce, the near-future trend of commerce, before others
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition, elements, and development of digital marketing. Similarities and differences, advantages and disadvantages of conventional and digital marketing
Hafta 2: Digital marketing advertising models – Banner, Search, Video & In-Stream, e-mail, etc.
Hafta 3: Consumer targeting models in digital marketing. PRACTICE: Creating a new campaign and determining a targeting method through the Google Ads panel
Hafta 4: Performance measurement parameters – Volume, Impression, Click, Conversion, Action. PRACTICE: Interpreting data from a real campaign
Hafta 5: Pricing and performance conversion metrics – CPM, CPC, CPA, etc. PRACTICE: Examining and interpreting performance metrics of a real campaign
Hafta 6: Supporting digital marketing applications (SMS, Mailing, WebPush, App Push)
Hafta 7: SEO – Search engine optimization: Definition of the concept, basic components
Hafta 9: (SERP) – Elements required to be listed in search engines (Title, Meta-Description, h1-h2-h3 headings, etc.)
Hafta 10: SEO analysis tools – Supporting applications (backlink, Domain Rating, etc.)

Hafta 11: Social media as a digital marketing channel. Influencer Marketing – Social Marketing and S-Commerce concepts. Performance measurement metrics such as interaction, likes, and shares (engagement)
Hafta 12: Facebook and Instagram Ads. Steps of creating a social media campaign. PRACTICE: Interpreting data from a real campaign
Hafta 13: Influencer marketing. S-Commerce concept and content management in social media (Content Marketing)
Hafta 14: Site analysis tools – Introduction to Google Analytics. PRACTICE: Interpreting site performance metrics of a real website

courses.name: Sign Language
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip university students with sign language skills to improve communication opportunities for hearing and speech-impaired people in society. It also aims to help students differentiate themselves in their future careers by developing practical communication skills in Turkish Sign Language.
courses.description: Introduction to the basic skills of producing and understanding Turkish Sign Language (TİD). The course covers manual alphabet and numbers, daily communication, grammar in context, asking and answering questions, conversation strategies, culturally appropriate behaviors, and interaction activities. Topics include deaf culture, greetings, self-introduction, pronouns, emotions, question forms, verbs, time, money, months, days, seasons, weather, family, colors, clothing, opposites, fruits, vegetables, and sentence practice.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate comprehensive mastery of target vocabulary items and high-frequency words in Turkish Sign Language at A1-A2 level.
Show awareness of TİD grammar, vocabulary, and phonology/cheironomy, including finger spelling practice.
Use familiar words, phrases, and sentences to present information to others in TİD.
Demonstrate receptive and productive skills in short dialogues and content-specific interactions.
Apply conversational strategies and interactional behaviors such as gaining attention and turn-taking.
Initiate, sustain, and conclude short conversations on familiar topics.
Use the language inside and outside the classroom for socializing and communication.
Read/watch and understand cultural information about Deaf Turkish culture, TİD, and its history.
Compare their own culture with Turkish Deaf culture and demonstrate intercultural awareness.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Hearing impairment and Deaf culture; alphabet
Hafta 2: Greeting dialogue; self-introduction in sign language; pronouns; facial expressions for questions; basic daily vocabulary and dialogues
Hafta 3: Positive and negative facial expressions; question examples; practice with learned vocabulary
Hafta 4: Emotions; example sentences with emotions

Hafta 9: Practice on time, clock, and money concepts; question facial expressions
Hafta 10: Months, days, seasons, weather events; verbs (10 verbs); practice with verbs

Hafta 12: Colors and clothes; clothing description
Hafta 13: Opposites; sentence construction practice with opposites
Hafta 14: Fruits, vegetables, other foods; sentence construction and sentence practice

courses.purpose: This course aims to familiarize students with scientific studies not only in their own field but also in other fields such as Physics, Chemistry, and Psychology. By focusing especially on interesting studies in the field of history, it seeks to increase students' interest in scientific research. It also examines some recent Nobel Prizes and the content of related works, encouraging students to generate ideas on these topics and think about the effects of scientific research results on the future of humanity.

learning_outcomes: Defines the concepts of science and scientific research.

Explains the scientific research process.

courses.name: Disaster and Emergency Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students from different disciplines to disaster management phases, crisis and risk management concepts, the roles and responsibilities of stakeholders in disaster management, disaster laws and regulations, and community-based organizational structures. It also covers practical applications such as risk, hazard, and SWOT analysis.
courses.description: The course covers what a disaster is, types and characteristics of disasters, the concept and principles of Integrated Disaster Management, disaster cycle phases, and the comparison of preparedness, response, mitigation, recovery, risk management, and crisis management. It also examines the economic dimensions of disasters, risk transfer methods such as disaster funds and insurance, SWOT/risk analysis, actors and their roles in disaster management, Turkey’s disaster management structure, laws, regulations, institutions, and examples of disaster management systems from Turkey and the world.
learning_outcomes: Understands the phases of disaster management and the concepts of crisis and risk management.
Recognizes the actors in disaster management and their authorities and responsibilities.
Learns risk transfer methods.
Knows the disaster management structure, laws, and regulations in Turkey.
Understands the Incident Command System and its applications.
Identifies risk, hazard, and SWOT analysis applications.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Concepts and definitions related to disasters (hazard, risk, what is a disaster? Difference between hazard and risk)
Hafta 2: Types and characteristics of disasters; is every hazard a disaster? What is Disaster Management? What are the basic principles of Disaster Management?

Hafta 3: What is the concept of Integrated Disaster Management (IDM)? What are its basic principles? What are the phases of IDM? Basic information about response, recovery, mitigation, and preparedness phases. Disaster cycle, risk and crisis management concepts
Hafta 4: Mitigation phase (economic dimensions of disasters, risk transfer methods: disaster funds and insurance, DASK example)

Hafta 5: Preparedness phase (early warning and rapid response, scenarios, emergency action plans); preparedness studies at institutional level, preparation of disaster plans, the place and importance of planning in disaster management, planning process, institutional planning examples
Hafta 6: Response phase (search and rescue, first aid, fire, security); drills - purpose, types of drills, and outcomes
Hafta 7: Incident Command System, establishment of institutional ICSs and their criteria
Hafta 9: Recovery / reconstruction phase (temporary and permanent housing, solving infrastructure problems, implementation of mitigation measures, current application examples)
Hafta 10: Disasters and development; disasters and gender; United Nations, Hyogo and Sendai Framework Action Plans
Hafta 11: What is SWOT analysis / risk analysis? How is it done?

Hafta 12: History of the disaster management system in Turkey, lessons learned, new regulations. Actors in disaster management and their roles (central and local governments, private sector, civil society organizations, and citizens). Disaster management structure, laws, regulations, and institutions in Turkey. Assessment after the 1999 and 2023 earthquakes
Hafta 13: Examples of disaster management systems from Turkey and the world, comparison; economic dimensions and effects of disasters, risk transfers and disaster funds
Hafta 14: Monitoring changes in knowledge, awareness, and development; course evaluation in terms of achievements

courses.name: Memory and Culture in New Media Ecology
courses.purpose: This course aims to focus on human memory through technological advances and the cultural transformations of contemporary society in digital networks. It is designed to embrace both theoretical arguments and narratives in the new media ecology through interdisciplinary perspectives that focus on the sociological, political, philosophical, ontological, and cultural trajectories of technology.
courses.description: This course discusses human memory through developments in technology and the cultural transformations of contemporary society in digital networks. It embraces theoretical arguments and narratives in New Media Ecology and Critical Memory studies through interdisciplinary perspectives, and examines the main debates and theoretical frameworks of digital society analyses. The course defines and analyzes the cultural trajectories of technology as well as the underlying epistemological, sociological, political, philosophical, and ontological assumptions of social research. It also examines the impact of digital culture and critically evaluates the role of technology in society and memory, investigating how digital media challenges traditional concepts of identity, community, body, politics, and personal relations.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to discuss the relationships between memory and the digital revolution.

Students will be able to analyze the digitalizing world by focusing on the sociological, political, philosophical, and cultural aspects of technology and networked popular culture in relation to memory and culture.
Students will understand specific concepts and terminology related to memory and culture in the New Media Ecology.

Students will be able to read and speak about specific topics related to the course content, such as artificial intelligence and hive minds, collective trauma, binding turns, cultural memory, cultural identity and ideologies, tangible and intangible memories, myths and digital narratives, media memory, hatred and forgiveness, healing discourses, and conflicting ideologies.
Students will be able to critically analyze and discuss memory and culture.

Students will be able to follow discussions on historical materialism, philosophy of history, the role of redemption, and peaceful discourse in digital media. They will also develop their own perspectives on the analysis of the post-truth era, presentism, and cynical attitudes toward history.

Students will be able to discuss various topics related to the futuristic aspects of memory, including astrobiology, transhumanism, posthumanism, cyborgs, anthropocentrism, neganthropocene, multi-planetary life, and cyberpunk.
Students will be able to talk about anthropocentric life from anthropocentrism to posthumanism.

Students will be able to read and speak about topics such as Big Data, Data Mining, Data Management, Data Surveillance, and Dystopia, while also exploring the dark sides of digital media historical narratives.
Students will improve their critical reading skills through their own interpretations, focusing on the cultural archaeology of popular digital culture and discourses on digital media.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course introduction. What are the benefits and harms of the digital, networked, information age? Retrospective analysis of the cultural meaning of technology.

Hafta 2: What is media ecology? Introduction to memory studies. Collective memory, the main components of collective memory, and cultural identity. A critical view of the digital age and culture. Main debates. Digital storytelling, media and technological determinism: broad economic, political, and cultural transitions as far-reaching as the early 19th-century Industrial Revolution. The emergence of urban print culture in the 15th century, the changing roles of reader and writer in interactive digital texts, and the inherently collaborative nature of digital narratives. Algorithms, the future of artificial intelligence. Günther Anders: the role of technology, Heidegger's Gestell, Bernard Stiegler on technique.

Hafta 3: Meta-level discussion of key terminology: hive mind, posthumanism, artificial intelligence, collective trauma, binding turns, myths, hatred, healing discourses, post-truth and conflicting ideologies, competitive memory, immanent subject, social media, hypermedia, post-memory, digital postmodernism, digital aesthetics, neuroscience, neuro-politics, neuropsychology, techno-communication, futurism, artificial consciousness, AI, VR, XR, MR, metaverse, transhumanism, posthumanism, cyborgs, anthropocentrism, neganthropocene, cyberpunk, big data, data mining, data management, dataveillance, dystopia.

Hafta 4: Assmann: individual, social, and cultural memory; analysis of media memory (pdf); media memory: theories and methodologies; Halbwachs' thought; Henri Bergson's philosophy; the Annales school of social and intellectual history: historians Marc Bloch and Lucien Febvre; cultural memory and early civilization: writing, remembrance, and political imagination - Jan Assmann.
Hafta 5: Media memory, ethics and testimony, new media memory, memory and digital media: six dynamics of the global memory field.
Hafta 6: Media memory and popular culture, media memory, journalism and journalistic practice, journalism as a forward-looking memory tool, archive, media, trauma.
Hafta 8: Archive, media, trauma, student analysis of digital media discourses and presentation on memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 9: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 10: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 11: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 12: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 13: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 14: Student presentations on the analysis of digital media discourses and memory and culture in the new media ecology.
Hafta 15: Evaluation of discussions on memory and culture in the context of New Media Ecology.

Understand the challenges and importance of gender equality in political participation.
Develop and express informed perspectives on current gender issues.

courses.name: Health Care in Multicultural Societies
courses.purpose: This course aims to help health science students and health professionals understand the effects of cultural differences on health care and develop the ability to provide patient care in a culturally sensitive manner. It also encourages respect for cultural diversity while equipping students with the knowledge and skills needed for effective communication and patient care.
courses.description: This course examines health inequalities among different cultures, the causes of differences in health outcomes, and strategies to reduce these inequalities. It provides cultural sensitivity training to improve communication with patients from different cultural backgrounds. The course also addresses immigrants' access to health services, the health problems of immigrant communities, and health policies specific to these communities. In addition, it explores traditional medical practices in different cultures and their interaction with modern medicine, helping students understand and integrate different treatment approaches.
learning_outcomes: Understand health inequalities and identify the reasons behind differences in health outcomes across cultures.
Develop cultural sensitivity skills to facilitate effective communication with patients from different cultural backgrounds.
Understand the challenges and barriers faced by immigrant populations in accessing health services.
Recognize and analyze health problems specific to immigrant communities and understand their unique health needs.
Examine and integrate traditional medical practices from different cultures with modern medical approaches.
Promote cultural diversity as a value and encourage an attitude of respect and appreciation for cultural differences.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Cultural Sensitivity and Communication Skills
Hafta 4: Effective Communication Practices and Scenarios
Hafta 5: Health Inequalities: Causes and Consequences
Hafta 6: The Impact of Cultural Differences on Health
Hafta 7: Access to Health Services for Immigrants
Hafta 9: Health Policies and Strategies for Immigrants
Hafta 10: Traditional Medical Practices and Diversity
Hafta 11: The Relationship Between Modern and Traditional Medicine
Hafta 12: Integration of Traditional and Modern Medical Practices
Hafta 13: Evaluation of Cultural Diversity and Conclusion
Hafta 14: Health Problems of Immigrant Communities
resources: Bhopal, R. S. (2007). Ethnicity, race, and health in multicultural societies: foundations for better epidemiology, public health, and health care. Oxford University Press.
Ritter, L. A., & Hoffman, N. (2009). Multicultural health. Jones & Bartlett Publishers.

courses.name: Principles of Cryptocurrency
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental principles of cryptocurrencies, including their structure, operation, and role in the digital economy. It also provides a theoretical framework for understanding blockchain-based financial systems and related social, technical, and regulatory issues.
courses.description: This course covers the definition and history of cryptocurrencies, their technical foundations, different types of crypto assets, decentralized finance (DeFi) projects, use cases, regulatory and legal issues, market analysis, portfolio management and risk factors, as well as the future of cryptocurrencies and their integration with business.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition and History of Cryptocurrencies
Hafta 8: Regulatory and Legal Issues of Cryptocurrencies
Hafta 10: Portfolio management and risk factors
Hafta 11: The Future of Cryptocurrencies and Integration with Business

courses.description: Definition of bioelectricity, electric properties of living beings, examples from plants, animal cells, organs, bioelectricity coordinated cell functions, bioelectricity linked diseases and bioelectricity based diagnostic and therapeutic applications.
