courses.name: Rising China: Power and Problems
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to analyze rising China and its influence in the regional and global order in terms of economic, military, and nuclear power, and to follow and discuss current global issues.
courses.description: This course provides undergraduate students with a broad understanding of China's political, economic, and strategic development in the modern era. The main discussions begin with the lowest point in China's history, when the country was transformed into a socialist state, and continue with China's contemporary rise and its effects on the world. The course addresses questions such as in what ways China is a rising country, how this rise has occurred, and how China's rise affects the United States and the global system.
learning_outcomes: Students will finish the course with basic knowledge about China Studies.
Students will gain critical thinking skills and a broad range of analytical tools to understand China's rise and its global impact.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Founding of the People's Republic of China and the Socialist Order
Hafta 2: The New World Order after the Cold War and China's Multipolarity Discourse

Hafta 10: China's Taiwan Policy and Problems
Hafta 11: China's Areas of Competition and Problems
Hafta 12: China's Regional and Global Influence

courses.name: Pharmaceutical Aspects of Nutritional Disorders
courses.purpose: To identify the role of common vitamins in normal physiology and disease, to examine the beneficial effects of vitamin supplementation, and to determine the potential toxic effects of vitamin supplementation.
courses.description: This course covers the central role of vitamins in nutrition. It introduces vitamin discoveries as factors preventing specific diseases and discusses their importance in health support, often due to their roles that are not highly specific.
learning_outcomes: Students are expected to acquire the following competencies: vitamins play a central role in nutrition. Vitamin discoveries as factors preventing specific diseases have marked the emergence of the field. They are also important because of their roles in health support, which are often not highly specific.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Perspectives on the Vitamin Concepts
Hafta 3: Considering the Individual Vitamins Water-soluble Vitamins- Vitamin C
Hafta 11: Using Current Knowledge of the Vitamins

courses.name: Management and Organization
courses.purpose: This course is designed to help students develop an understanding and awareness of the fundamentals of management and the behavioral patterns of organizations, provide a general overview of the field of management, and offer insights into the concept of organizational structure. By understanding how management theory has evolved and exploring the four basic functions of management—planning, organizing, leading, and controlling—students will learn how organizations use scarce resources to achieve their goals.
courses.description: This course covers basic management and business administration concepts; organisational environment and its variables, the stages of management in a historical process and its reflections to the present, managerial functions and current management practices.
learning_outcomes: Knows management and related concepts, can explain the relationships between them
Understand the evolutionary process of management theories and make connections
Explains the main components of the internal and external environments of business, their impact, importance and influence on an organisation.
Explains how decisions are made in an organisation, including the types of decisions to be made, problem definition and various decision-making approaches.
Understands management functions
Defines basic business concepts, principles and values that contribute to organisational performance.
Reveals the subsystems within the business and the relationships between them
Knows post-modern management concepts
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ders Hakkında ve Temel Kavramlar
Hafta 2: Yönetim Teorileri: Kalsik Yönetim Teorisi
Hafta 3: Management Theories: Classical Approach (Scientific, Management Process, Bureaucracy)
Hafta 5: Neo-classical management approach: Behavioural Management Theory, Hawthorne studies, Kurt Levin Leadership Studies, C. Agyris- Mature and Immature Human Model,
Hafta 10: Yönetim Fonksiyonları: Yönlendirme ve Liderlik

courses.name: Decision Making and Problem Solving Techniques
courses.purpose: This course examines the decision-making process with close attention to basic decision types, tools, methods, and insights. It aims to help students understand how decision making involves both logic and emotion and to equip them with practical tools to identify decision types, select appropriate methods, and engage teams effectively in the process.
courses.description: This course introduces decision making as a core managerial function and a process of choosing among alternatives to achieve a goal. It covers normative, prescriptive, and descriptive theories of judgment and choice; models of decision-making processes; the effects of context, experience, memory, and knowledge on decisions; biases and heuristics; and real-world applications of these topics.
learning_outcomes: Learn the basic concepts and terminology of decision theory.
Learn to structure, model, and classify decision-making problems.
Learn how to deal with uncertainty and risk in the decision-making process.
Explain the qualitative limitations of managerial decision making and propose strategies to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of decisions.
Generate and evaluate alternative solutions.
Apply critical and creative thinking to decision-making processes.
Consider ethical issues in the decision-making process.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation to the course / Introduction to Decision Making and Problem Solving
Hafta 2: Cognitive Foundations of Decision Making
Hafta 3: Rational and Intuitive Decision-Making Models (Classical Models and Bounded Rationality / Intuition in Problem Solving)
Hafta 4: Problem Identification and Structuring (Clearly Defining Problems / Tools for Structuring Complex Problems)
Hafta 5: Biases and Heuristics (Strategies for Reducing Bias in Decision Making)
Hafta 7: Creative Thinking and Innovation Tools
Hafta 8: Group Decision Making and Team Problem Solving / Selecting and Evaluating Solutions
Hafta 9: Quantitative Tools in Decision Making (Decision Trees, Payoff Matrix, SWOT Analysis, Cost-Benefit Analysis)
Hafta 10: Conflict Resolution and Negotiation in Decision Making
Hafta 12: Decision Making Under Uncertainty and Risk
Hafta 13: Integrating Decision Making and Problem Solving in Organizations
Hafta 14: Organizational Decision Making and Leadership

courses.name: Management in Different Cultures
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to manage, communicate, and lead effectively in different cultural settings, address ethical challenges, and develop strategies for global partnerships and assignments.
courses.description: This course provides an in-depth understanding of how cultural differences affect management practices and organizational behavior in a globalized world. Using case studies, discussions, and practical examples, it examines the key concepts of intercultural communication, cross-cultural management, and global leadership. Students will study cultural frameworks, analyze management challenges in different cultural contexts, and develop effective decision-making and problem-solving strategies in diverse environments. The course emphasizes the importance of cultural awareness and adaptability in achieving organizational success in international settings.
learning_outcomes: Identify and analyze cultural environments and their effects on organizational practices.
Communicate effectively in cross-cultural settings.
Understand global leadership principles and adapt leadership styles to different cultural contexts.
Evaluate ethical dilemmas and social responsibilities in managing international organizations.
Develop strategies for successful partnerships and negotiations in diverse cultural settings.
Prepare for global assignments by creating management plans that address cultural challenges.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course, syllabus, and expectations. Introduction to Management in Different Cultures.
Hafta 2: Global Managers in a Changing World
Hafta 9: Management Ethics and Social Responsibility
Hafta 11: Global Teams and Global Assignments
Hafta 12: Critical Issues in Cross-Cultural Management
resources: Steers, R., M. 2020. Management Across Cultures, 4. Ed., Cambridge University Press.
Wildman, J. L., Griffith, R. L., Armon, B. K. 2016. Critical Issues in Cross Cultural Management, Springer.

courses.description: This course introduces the basic concepts of consumer behavior and aims to help students understand the effects of culture and subcultures on individuals' purchasing behavior. The brand management process is examined in all its stages.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the effects of various factors on the consumer decision process with their outcomes.
Explain the consumer purchasing decision process.
Explain the basic concepts and models of consumer behavior.

courses.name: Innovative Strategies for New Product Development
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the skills needed to make informed decisions about which new products to develop and which strategies to use in the process. It builds a conceptual framework and connects it with real-life examples to provide a solid foundation for designing new product strategies and development processes.
courses.description: The course focuses on researching opportunities, developing strategies, and designing the processes of creating new products. It emphasizes the high-risk nature of new product development and aims to increase the chances of success for new products by examining the tools, techniques, and managerial issues involved in the process.
learning_outcomes: Understand the strategic importance of continuous innovation and new product/service development across different market areas and sectors.
Develop an in-depth understanding of innovation and new product development processes.
Analyze market needs and opportunities and evaluate this information.
Apply processes to generate new product and service ideas.
Master the concepts and tools required for new product development through case studies and projects.
Appreciate the importance of design and design thinking in the product development process.
Gain the ability to evaluate and test new product concepts.
Develop strategies for bringing new products to market.
Design and develop their own new product or service and prepare a launch plan, experiencing the new product development process in practice.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Students learn the basics of identifying and selecting market opportunities, focusing on unmet needs and gaps across different sectors.
Hafta 2: The week continues with deeper analysis of potential market areas, developing skills in evaluating market needs and understanding customer problems.
Hafta 3: Methods for creating product concepts suitable for identified opportunities are introduced, including brainstorming and idea development strategies.
Hafta 4: Students expand idea generation methods and develop initial product ideas, emphasizing alignment with market needs and business goals.
Hafta 5: Students learn to evaluate product concepts in terms of feasibility, market appeal, and strategic fit, using techniques such as SWOT and cost-benefit analysis.
Hafta 6: Building on the previous week, students focus on deeper evaluation of product ideas, including risk analysis and alignment with organizational goals.
Hafta 7: An applied case study culminates the concept evaluation phase, allowing students to practice evaluating a real-world product concept.
Hafta 8: Students present their projects showcasing the product concepts developed through opportunity identification, concept creation, and evaluation.
Hafta 9: Project presentations continue, allowing more students to present their work and receive feedback.
Hafta 10: Students learn how to bring product concepts closer to reality through prototyping, testing, and using feedback to improve the product.
Hafta 11: The development phase is deepened with topics such as user testing, design adjustments, and production planning.
Hafta 12: The course focuses on strategies for successfully launching a new product, including timing, positioning, and marketing tactics.
Hafta 13: The final section prepares students for the practicalities of introducing a new product, focusing on positioning and market entry strategies.
Hafta 14: Students present group projects demonstrating the full product development process from opportunity identification to launch.
resources: New Products Management, 12th ed., by Merle Crawford and Anthony Di Benedetto, McGraw-Hill

courses.name: Modern Leadership Theories
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of historical and contemporary leadership theories and their practical applications in modern organizations. By examining transformational, servant, authentic, adaptive, digital, creative, and other emerging leadership approaches, students will develop analytical skills to evaluate leadership effectiveness across different cultural and organizational contexts. The course emphasizes experiential learning through case analyses, projects, and self-assessments, enabling students to critically assess their own leadership styles and acquire the competencies needed to lead ethically, inclusively, and innovatively in dynamic environments.
courses.description: This course examines the historical development of foundational leadership theories, contemporary leadership theories, and their practical applications in today’s dynamic and globalized business environment. It aims to develop students’ critical understanding of leadership through theoretical foundations, self-assessments, real-world case analyses, and experiential projects.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and compare major modern leadership theories.
Apply leadership theories to organizational problems.
Evaluate leadership effectiveness in different cultural and situational contexts.
Demonstrate leadership competencies through team-based projects.
Reflect on their own leadership styles and development paths.
Reach original conclusions by using the interdisciplinary nature of the field.
Position the field of leadership within the broader area of social sciences and identify its interdisciplinary relations.
Follow developments in the field of leadership.
Develop strategies and plans for modern leadership styles.
Contribute to academic and applied studies in the field of leadership.
Define the ethical values of the field and be aware of issues such as the universality of social rights, social justice, quality, and cultural values.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and Definition of Leadership
Hafta 2: Evolution of Leadership Thought – Early Theories – Trait-Based / Inborn Theories (Great Man, Trait Theory)
Hafta 3: Classical Theories – Behavioral and Contingency Theories (Ohio & Michigan Schools, Lewin, Early Charismatic, Contingency, Path-Goal, LMX)
Hafta 5: Value- and Change-Oriented Theories (Transactional, Laissez-Faire, Servant, Transformational)
Hafta 7: New Generation Theories (Adaptive, Emotional Intelligence, Distributed / Shared, Authentic, Cross-Cultural, Ethical / Responsible, Complexity)
Hafta 8: The Difference Between Leadership and Management
Hafta 9: Leadership Styles in Different Sectors and Business Types
Hafta 10: New Leadership Approaches Reflecting the 21st Century: Workplace Trends (Creative, Digital, Inclusive Leadership)
Hafta 11: Leadership Role in Crisis Management, Case Studies
Hafta 12: Leadership in the Age of Artificial Intelligence (AI)
Hafta 14: How Have New Business Dynamics Changed Leadership Characteristics?

courses.name: Turkish Cinema
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand Turkish cinema by presenting its historical development. It also seeks to enable students to interpret contemporary films by revealing their historical background and foundations.
courses.description: This course examines the historical development of Turkish cinema from its emergence to the present. It covers the phases shaped by technological, social, and political developments and analyzes various cinema movements to show the changes in cinematic language and narration.

Recall information about the beginnings of cinema in Turkey.
Recall information about the Theater People, Transition, and Filmmakers periods.
Recall the Yeşilçam period, film production processes, and the functioning of the industry.
Interpret prominent directors and films from the beginnings to the present.

courses.name: Short Film
courses.purpose: This course focuses on film production with an emphasis on short films. It is designed to introduce students to the basic characteristics of the short film format, show the relationship between screenplay and film style, provide information about all stages of filmmaking, and introduce independent filmmaking methods.
courses.description: This course is an introduction to short film. Students will learn about the characteristics of short films, basic film grammar, film styles, and all stages of short film production. Although the main course materials will be uploaded to the Blackboard system, students are advised to take their own notes during classes.
learning_outcomes: Narrative Structure Mastery: Students will demonstrate a deep understanding of various narrative structures used in short films, including linear, non-linear, and experimental formats.

Style Analysis: Students will critically analyze different stylistic approaches in short films and recognize the contribution of elements such as cinematography, editing, sound design, and mise-en-scène to the overall narrative and emotional impact.

courses.name: Journey to the Micro World of Cells!
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fascinating and diverse world of microorganisms and their important roles in nature and human life. Through a combination of theoretical knowledge and presentations, students learn microbial diversity and function, explore cellular structures and processes, understand basic microscopy and imaging techniques, examine the human microbiome, and connect the micro world to real-world issues.
courses.description: The course covers an introduction to the micro world, cell biology, bacteria and archaea, viruses, fungi and protists, the human microbiome, and microorganisms and the environment. It also includes student presentations and practical observation activities such as microscopy and micrograph examination.
learning_outcomes: Explain microbial diversity and subcellular structures, including the roles of bacteria, fungi, protists, and viruses in ecosystems, human health, and disease.
Distinguish between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, including their structures and functions, especially cellular organelles, membranes, and genetic material.
Identify microscopy techniques and imaging tools used to observe and analyze microorganisms, cellular structures, and biological samples.
Examine the importance of the human microbiome, especially the gut microbiome, and explain how antibiotics and probiotics affect human health, disease, and treatment.
Evaluate real-world applications of microbiological concepts in environmental sustainability, biotechnology, public health, and epidemiology.
Work effectively in groups to research, discuss, and present findings on topics related to microbial biology, genetic engineering, and the microbiome.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the Micro World; overview of the micro world, importance of microorganisms in nature and human life, introduction to microscopy and imaging technologies; practice: introduction to microscopy techniques and micrograph observation of basic samples (onion cells, pond water)

Hafta 2: Cell Biology; structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, functions of cellular organelles, cell membranes and transport mechanisms; practice: micrograph examination of plant and animal cells

Hafta 3: Bacteria and Archaea; introduction to bacteria and archaea, structure and function of bacterial cells, roles of bacteria in ecosystems (nitrogen fixation, decomposition); practice: exploring bacterial cultures on agar plates and examining bacterial diversity with micrographs

Hafta 4: Viruses – Life at the Edge; structure of viruses, how viruses reproduce: lytic and lysogenic cycles, role of viruses in disease and genetic engineering; practice: discussing historical pandemics caused by viruses

Hafta 5: Fungi and Protists; introduction to fungi: yeasts, molds, and mushrooms, protists: diversity and habitats, ecological importance of fungi and protists; practice: observing fungal growth and examining protists from water samples
Hafta 6: The Human Microbiome; microorganisms living in and on the human body, role of the gut microbiome in health and disease, effects of antibiotics and probiotics

Hafta 7: Microorganisms and the Environment; microbial roles in biogeochemical cycles (carbon, nitrogen, sulfur), microbial decomposition and waste management, bioremediation: using microorganisms to clean pollution
resources: Molecular Biology of the Cell by Bruce Alberts, Alexander D. Johnson, Julian Lewis, and Martin Raff
Microbiology: An Introduction by Gerard J. Tortora, Berdell R. Funke, and Christine L. Case
Principles of Virology by Jane Flint, Vincent R. Racaniello, Glenn F. Rall, and Anna Marie Skalka
The Human Microbiome: A New Frontier in Health and Disease by Julian R. Marchesi and Jacques Ravel

courses.name: Digital Leadership
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce digital media literacy by providing students with the skills to access, evaluate, and create digital media content, while also developing their critical thinking skills by focusing on political, social, and legal issues that are heavily discussed in the new media environment.
courses.description: This course covers the basic principles of digital media literacy, legal rights and digital surveillance, information literacy and digital manipulation, online politics, and big data.
learning_outcomes: Develops the ability to evaluate and create digital media content
Follows and critically approaches political, social, and legal issues that are heavily discussed in the new media environment

Hafta 2: History and Development of Digital Media
Hafta 3: Media Literacy in the Digital Age
Hafta 4: Digital Democracy and Citizen Participation

Hafta 6: Surveillance in the Digital Age - Privacy of Online Identity
Hafta 7: Information Literacy in the Post-Truth Era - Fake News and Digital Manipulation

Hafta 13: Student Presentations and Case Studies - Presentation of students' research on specific topics

courses.name: Turkish Business Environment
courses.purpose: To provide students with a comprehensive understanding of Turkey's business world and economic environment. It aims to help students understand Turkey's economic dynamics, the opportunities and threats encountered in business life, and become familiar with the local business culture.
courses.description: Turkey's economic structure; the basic dynamics of business life; sector analysis and prominent sectors; investment opportunities in Turkey; local business culture and values; international trade and Turkey; analysis of economic indicators.
learning_outcomes: To have knowledge about Turkey's business environment
To understand the local business culture
To analyze economic indicators
To recognize sectors in Turkey and evaluate investment opportunities

Hafta 2: Turkey’s Economic Structure and Basic Dynamics
Hafta 3: Sector Analysis in Turkey: Agriculture, Industry and Services

courses.name: Global Economy and New Trends
courses.purpose: To provide an understanding of global issues and countries in light of historical, economic, commercial, political, social, geographical, and religious backgrounds and current developments; to make inferences about likely future markets and international trade topics; and to develop policy recommendations based on these inferences.
courses.description: This course addresses world political and technology history, international competition, and global economic dynamics. It examines wars that have shaped the world, current disputes, areas of cooperation, the influence of religion, the developing African economy, and current international economic, political, social, and commercial developments, while analyzing international relations.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to analyze the effects of world political and technology history on global developments.
Students will be able to explain international competition and foreign trade theories and evaluate economic dynamics among countries.
Students will be able to interpret wars that have shaped the world, current international disputes, and the effects of religion on trade and social life in a historical context.
Students will be able to analyze data on world trade volume and the developing African economy.
Students will gain insight into the effects of social media on daily life, the economy, and trade.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: The Evolution of the World Economy in Historical Process
Hafta 3: The World Economy by Countries
Hafta 4: The World Economy by Countries
Hafta 5: Wars Shaping the World, Current Disputes
Hafta 7: Current Economic Developments (Trump, Trade Wars)
Hafta 11: Mega Projects by Countries on a Global Scale

Acemoglu, Daron, and James A. Robinson. Why Nations Fail: The Origins of Power, Prosperity, and Poverty. New York: Crown Business, 2012.
Krugman, Paul R., Maurice Obstfeld, and Marc Melitz. International Economics: Theory and Policy. 11th ed. Boston: Pearson, 2018.

courses.name: Polar Realities: Social Science Insights into Extreme Environments
Migration and Social Order
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students learn the basic concepts and theories of international migration and understand the historical processes and debates in this field. It also covers migration processes in different regions, international migration policies, and the economic and social dimensions of migration.
courses.description: After discussing the basic theories and concepts of international migration, the course explains the historical development of migration and the relationship between globalization and migration. The course also examines forced migrants, irregular migrants, and environmental migration, and discusses international labor migration as well as the migration policies of Europe and Turkey.
learning_outcomes: Understand international migration and related concepts
Understand international migration theories
Understand the historical background of international migration
Evaluate the economic and social aspects of international migration
Discuss migration policies
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Conceptual Framework of International Migration
Hafta 7: International Labor Migration and the Global Economy
Hafta 10: Integration of Migrants and Conceptual Debates
resources: Stephen Castles and Mark J. Miller, The Age of Migration: International Population Movements in the Modern World, Macmillan Press, 1993 (Fifth Edition, 2014).
Khalid Kosher, International Migration: A Very Short Introduction, Oxford, 2016.

courses.name: Global Climate Changes and Green Transformation
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the scientific foundations of climate change and the policy and economic mechanisms that drive the transition to a low-carbon economy. It aims to build a basic understanding of international climate governance, carbon pricing, and green transformation policies.
courses.description: This course examines global climate change from scientific, economic, and policy perspectives. It covers the causes and impacts of climate change, market failures, and the transition to a low-carbon economy. The course also analyzes international climate governance, carbon pricing mechanisms, and emissions trading systems, with special emphasis on the European Green Deal, CBAM, and their effects on international trade and green transformation.
learning_outcomes: Explains the scientific foundations and main causes of global climate change.
Analyzes the economic rationale of climate policies, including market failures and carbon pricing mechanisms.
Evaluates the effects of climate change on sectors, trade, and economic development.
Evaluates international climate agreements and climate governance frameworks.
Interprets the European Green Deal, CBAM, and emissions trading systems in the context of green transformation.

Hafta 2: Climate Science and the Global Climate System
Hafta 3: Natural and Anthropogenic Causes of Climate Change
Hafta 4: Economics of Climate Change: Market Failures and Risk
Hafta 5: Global and National Impacts of Climate Change
Hafta 6: Energy Transition and the Low-Carbon Economy
Hafta 8: International Climate Agreements and Global Governance
Hafta 9: Carbon Pricing and Emissions Trading Systems
Hafta 10: Green Transformation and Structural Change
Hafta 11: Green Finance and Sustainable Investment
Hafta 12: EU Green Deal and CBAM
Hafta 13: Trade Effects of the EU Green Deal
Hafta 14: Climate Law and National Emissions Trading Systems

courses.name: European Politics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a broad perspective on the European Union and its place in the world. By the end of the course, students should be able to discuss contemporary European Union policies and evaluate current debates related to the European Union.
courses.description: This course explains the historical development, institutions, and structure of the European Union. It addresses the main issues shaping contemporary European politics, the role of Europe in the world, and the future of European integration.
learning_outcomes: Understand the historical development and evolution of the European Union
Evaluate the main debates in contemporary European politics
Analyze developments in contemporary European politics from a critical perspective
Develop a comprehensive understanding of the challenges and hopes facing European integration
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: History of the European Union
Hafta 3: Institutions of the European Union
Hafta 4: European Union Policies: Single Market and Monetary Union Policy
Hafta 9: European Union Security and Global Power
Hafta 10: European Union Migration and Asylum Policies
Hafta 11: The European Right, The European Left
Hafta 13: The Future of the European Union
resources: Herman Lelieveldt & Sebastiaan Princen, The Politics of The European Union, Cambridge University Press, 2022.

courses.name: Sociology of Beliefs and Cross-Cultural Studies
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the complex relationships between belief systems and cultural structures from a sociological perspective. It also encourages students to analyze how different societies form, interpret, and practice beliefs, and how cultural norms, values, and social structures shape those beliefs.
courses.description: This course examines the complex relationship between belief systems and cultural structures from a sociological perspective. Students will investigate how various societies form, interpret, and practice beliefs, and how cultural norms, values, and social structures influence them. Through cross-cultural analyses, the course aims to develop a comprehensive understanding of the diversity and commonalities of belief systems around the world. Students will critically evaluate the role of beliefs in shaping individual identities and social dynamics through lectures, discussions, and case studies.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the interaction between belief systems and cultural structures.
Apply sociological theories to belief systems.
Make cross-cultural comparisons.
Evaluate the impact of beliefs on social stratification.
Examine the role of religion in contemporary society.
Develop cultural competence.
Develop critical thinking and reflect on their own belief systems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the basic concepts of sociology and the sociological imagination.
Hafta 2: The relationship between culture and social structure.
Hafta 3: Sociological analysis of belief systems. Students are informed about the formation and continuity processes of belief systems.
Hafta 4: Social functions of religion and secularization processes.
Hafta 6: Identity and social belonging in a cultural context.
Hafta 9: The impact of belief systems on social change.
Hafta 10: Modernization, globalization, and cultural change processes.
Hafta 11: The relationship between belief systems and social inequalities.
Hafta 12: The impact of religion on political behavior and public culture.
Hafta 13: Social effects of religious institutions through case studies.
Hafta 14: General review of the topics discussed throughout the course.
Hafta 15: Final exam. Students are given 5 questions to answer at home and are expected to combine what they learned in class with their own life experiences.
resources: Handbook of the Sociology of Religion edited by Michele Dillon
Lecturer notes, powerpoint slides and videos

courses.name: Pharmaceutical Aspects of Nutritional Disorders
courses.purpose: To define the role of common vitamins in normal physiology and disease, to examine the beneficial effects of vitamin supplements, and to identify the potential toxic effects of vitamin supplementation.
courses.description: This course covers the central role of vitamins in nutrition. It introduces vitamin discoveries as factors that prevent specific diseases and discusses their importance in health support, often due to their lack of specificity in action.
learning_outcomes: Students are expected to acquire the following competencies: Vitamins play a central role in nutrition.
Vitamin discoveries as factors preventing specific diseases have marked the emergence of the field.
They are also important because of their roles in health support, which often lack the specificity that facilitates their discovery.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Perspectives on the Vitamin Concepts
Hafta 3: Considering the Individual Vitamins Water-soluble Vitamins- Vitamin C
Hafta 11: Using Current Knowledge of the Vitamins
resources: The Vitamins: Fundamental Aspects in Nutrition and Health. 4th edition 2012, Elsevier, UK.
Vitamins and Minerals Demystified: A self-teaching Guide. 2011, New York: McGraw Hill Professional.

courses.name: Health Promotion
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with basic knowledge about protecting and improving the health of individuals, families, and community groups, to help them gain healthy lifestyle behaviors, and to develop basic knowledge about planning, implementing, and evaluating behavior change strategies.
courses.description: This course covers the definition, importance, and historical development of the concept of health promotion, health promotion models, practices related to health promotion in our country, the duties of the midwife, and applications carried out in different areas aimed at health promotion.
learning_outcomes: Explain the concepts related to health and health promotion.
Define the conditions and risk factors affecting health.
Explain the role and responsibilities of the midwife in health promotion.
Explain programs and practices aimed at health promotion.
weekly_topics: Hafta 14: Early diagnosis and its importance

courses.name: AI and Transformation Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to fundamental artificial intelligence technologies and their applications in organizations, examine how AI drives organizational transformation and change management, develop students' ability to analyze, plan, and implement AI-focused initiatives, explore the ethical, social, and operational challenges of AI adoption, enhance critical thinking and problem-solving skills in AI-enabled business contexts, and prepare students to communicate AI strategies and transformation plans effectively.
courses.description: This course examines the role of artificial intelligence in organizational transformation and change. Students explore how AI technologies affect strategy, operations, and decision-making processes while also considering ethical, managerial, and human factors. The course combines theoretical foundations with practical applications to prepare students to manage AI-enabled transformations effectively.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental concepts and technologies of AI in business and management contexts.
Analyze the effects of AI on organizational processes and strategies.
Apply change management principles in AI-driven transformations.
Identify challenges and risks in AI adoption, considering ethical, social, and operational dimensions.
Develop practical solutions for implementing AI initiatives in organizations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and Transformation Management (definitions, scope, and importance of AI in organizations)
Hafta 2: AI Technologies and Capabilities (machine learning, natural language processing, robotics, and automation)
Hafta 3: AI in Strategic Management (aligning AI initiatives with organizational strategy)
Hafta 4: Organizational Change and Transformation (Kotter, ADKAR, Lewin)
Hafta 5: Challenges and Barriers to AI Adoption (resistance to change, skill gaps, technology-related stress, and infrastructure requirements)
Hafta 7: Data Management and AI Ethics (data quality, privacy, bias, and responsible AI practices)
Hafta 8: AI-Supported Decision Making (augmented intelligence, AI use in operational and strategic decisions)
Hafta 9: AI Implementation Planning (project management, stakeholder engagement, and risk assessment)
Hafta 10: Human Factors in AI Transformation (employee engagement, training, and cultural change)
Hafta 11: AI in Digital Transformation (integration with other technologies: IoT, cloud, analytics)
resources: Lecturer's Notes
Selected articles and case studies (provided throughout the course)

courses.name: AI and Transformation Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip participants with the knowledge and tools to understand how technology transforms industries, societies, and individuals. It also seeks to develop the ability to identify opportunities and address challenges in technology-driven change processes. The course encourages critical evaluation of the ethical, social, and political consequences of artificial intelligence and builds awareness of its interdisciplinary impact.
courses.description: This course examines technological transformation from the Industrial Revolutions to Artificial Intelligence (AI) and addresses the fundamental concepts of transformation management. Students will analyze the role of AI in different disciplines and discuss its social, political, and ethical effects. In addition, guest speakers from various sectors will share real-world experiences of AI's impact, offering practical perspectives on technology-driven change.
learning_outcomes: Understand historical and contemporary technological transformations.
Identify the fundamental principles of transformation management in technology-driven change.
Evaluate the opportunities and challenges that artificial intelligence offers in different sectors.
Critically analyze the business, social, ethical, and political impacts of artificial intelligence.
Apply transformation management strategies to real-world scenarios.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the AI and Transformation Management course
Hafta 3: Forces and Challenges of Technological Transformation
Hafta 4: Transformation Management – Alignment with Strategy
Hafta 5: Innovation Management - Problem Solving in the Transformation Process
Hafta 6: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence – Concepts, History, and Trends
Hafta 7: Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
Hafta 9: Challenges of Scaling AI in Organizations (Panel Discussions)
Hafta 13: The Role of People in a Transforming World
Hafta 14: Artificial Intelligence and Its Evolving Face

courses.name: Political Literacy
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to understand political life together with its structures, institutions, and actors.
courses.description: This course aims to help students understand political systems, ideologies, political life and institutions, key political actors, civic participation, media literacy, public opinion, and the impact of global issues.
learning_outcomes: Understand basic political concepts and structures
Analyze the functions of the political system
Recognize the different political ideologies and political outcomes of different political institutions and actors
Critically evaluate political arguments and processes
Develop citizenship awareness and democratic participation skills
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to political literacy: course overview, importance of political literacy, basic concepts and terminology, expectations and assessments.
Hafta 2: Types of political systems, historical developments, separation of powers, and checks and balances mechanisms
Hafta 3: Political ideologies: yesterday and today; political ethics and political polarization
Hafta 4: Civic culture and advocacy: the role of media and media literacy

courses.name: Communicating Sustainability: Building a Better Future
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the role of communication and marketing in achieving sustainability goals, develop effective and ethical sustainability communication strategies, analyze global sustainability campaigns, identify greenwashing practices, examine industry-led initiatives such as Ad Net Zero, design a sustainability-focused campaign, and apply crisis communication strategies in sustainability-related brand crises.
courses.description: This course examines topics at the intersection of communication, marketing, and sustainability, focusing on how brands and organizations can create ethical, transparent, and effective sustainability communication strategies. It combines marketing fundamentals, sustainability principles, crisis communication, and industry best practices to provide students with a strategic perspective on responsible advertising and brand management. The course covers integrated marketing communication (IMC), sustainability communication, greenwashing, sectoral initiatives such as Ad Net Zero, crisis communication and reputation management, ethical marketing and consumer trust, and applied project development for a sustainability-focused communication plan. It uses interactive discussions, real case analyses, and project-based learning.
learning_outcomes: Critically evaluate sustainability-themed campaigns.
Identify false or misleading sustainability claims (greenwashing).
Develop responsible communication strategies by applying ethical advertising principles.

Hafta 3: Fundamentals of Integrated Marketing Communication Part 2

Hafta 11: Examples of Sustainability Communication, Part 2 - Analysis of successful sustainability campaigns. Activity: Analyze effective messaging strategies in well-executed sustainability campaigns.

Hafta 12: Sustainability Crisis Communication and Reputation Management Topics: - How brands recover from sustainability-related crises Activity and assignment: Develop sustainability crisis management for a fictional case
Hafta 13: Project Briefings Topics: Debriefings regarding students' final project, expectations, and assessment.
Hafta 14: Project Group Presentations - Group work on strategies Instructor feedback on project concepts
Hafta 15: Project Group Presentations - Group work on strategies Instructor feedback on project concepts

courses.name: E Commerce
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the technological, social, and functional dimensions of electronic commerce and provide students with a comprehensive understanding of business models, marketing strategies, consumer behavior, and the ethical and legal aspects of e-commerce in the digital economy. Through current examples and case studies, the course also seeks to develop students' strategic thinking skills in the field of digital commerce.
courses.description: Definition, scope, and development of electronic commerce; digital business models and online value creation strategies; e-commerce infrastructure including hardware, software, internet, and mobile systems; electronic payment systems and digital security; digital marketing including SEO, SEM, social media, and email marketing; online consumer behavior and customer relationship management (CRM); subscription-based business models and user-generated content (UGC); ethical, legal, and social aspects of e-commerce; B2B, B2C, C2C, and C2B e-commerce models; mobile commerce (m-commerce) and next-generation applications; international e-commerce and cross-border digital marketing strategies; discussions through case analyses and current examples.
learning_outcomes: Defines the basic components, business models, and current trends of e-commerce.
Analyzes e-commerce business models and strategies.
Explains e-commerce marketing and online consumer behavior.
Discusses legal, security, and ethical issues in e-commerce.
Applies digital marketing strategies such as SEO, SEM, and social media marketing.
Evaluates the social, mobile, and local marketing dimensions of e-commerce.

courses.name: Brand Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamental concepts, strategies, and practices of branding. It also seeks to develop the analytical and decision-making skills needed for successful brand management by covering topics such as brand identity, brand positioning, brand equity, brand communication, and consumer perception. In addition, students will gain an understanding of the brand-building process through national and international examples.
courses.description: Branding and the marketing mix, building strong brands, marketing and communication programs, measuring brand performance, brand audits, brand design and structures, and managing brand equity for long-term success.
learning_outcomes: Learn relevant concepts
Understand product and brand management concepts
Comprehend strategies such as brand awareness and positioning in the global market environment
Understand sustainable brand management in a market environment where cultural differences are considered
Understand changing trends and technologies to improve brand management skills
Understand branding and relate concepts through case examples
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Definition of the brand, positioning, market segmentation, consumer behavior
Hafta 4: Brand loyalty, perceived quality, brand identity
Hafta 6: Brand equity and brand extension
resources: Strategic Brand Management: Building, Measuring and Managing Brand Equity, 5th Global Edition, Kevin Lane Keller and Vanitha Swaminathan

courses.name: Scientific community and communication
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to understand, analyze, and apply communication within the scientific community and between science and society. It also addresses the ethical and social dimensions of science communication while ensuring that scientific knowledge is conveyed to the public in a clear, accurate, and effective way.
courses.description: This course examines the dynamics of communication within the scientific community and between science, media, and society. Students will learn the historical development of science communication, the roles of key actors (scientists, media, institutions, and religious organizations), and the functions of science communication in everyday life and during crises. In addition to the theoretical framework, the course includes practical activities such as science journalism, storytelling, interviews, social media content production, and crisis communication.
learning_outcomes: Explain the definition, history, and main purposes of science communication.
Analyze the key actors involved in science communication, including scientists, media, the public, and religious institutions.
Evaluate the role of science communication during crises such as pandemics and disasters, and analyze case studies.
Compare the ways scientific information is conveyed in mass media such as TV, the internet, and social media.
Simplify scientific content for target audiences and present it through news, interviews, and storytelling techniques.
Transform scientific information into effective content for digital platforms such as Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and infographics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is Science?, Introduction to Science
Hafta 2: Science in the History of Civilization-1 (Chinese Civilization)
Hafta 3: Science in the History of Civilization-2 (Science in Ancient Greece and the Islamic World)
Hafta 4: Science in the History of Civilization-3 (Science in the Middle Ages of Europe and the Enlightenment Period)
Hafta 5: Science in the History of Civilization-4 (Industrial Revolution, the 20th Century, and the Age of Artificial Intelligence)
Hafta 6: Institutionalization of Science and Scientific Communities
Hafta 7: Science Culture (Science Psychology and Science Sociology)
Hafta 11: Digital media use of science communication stakeholders
Hafta 12: Communication strategies with the media for scientists-1
Hafta 13: Communication strategies with the media for scientists-2
resources: Lecture notes
Ders Notları- lecture notes

courses.name: AI for Everyone
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students from different disciplines understand artificial intelligence at a conceptual level, use it practically in relation to their own fields, and develop a critical perspective on its ethical, legal, and social impacts.
courses.description: The course introduces AI from fundamental concepts to current applications such as Generative AI, and prepares students for the future AI world through ethics, practical tools, and strategic transformation topics.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic concepts of artificial intelligence (ML, DL, NLP, vision) in non-technical language.
Develop awareness of the social, ethical, and legal dimensions of artificial intelligence.
Create a prototype using two designated no-code tools, one visual and one text or generative AI based.
Analyze a problem within their own discipline (Law, Business, Art, etc.) using an AI approach.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction: What is AI, Why is it Important? (Basic Concepts, AI History and Scope)
Hafta 2: Data: The Fuel of Artificial Intelligence (Data Types, Collection, Quality, and Data Literacy)
Hafta 3: How Does AI "Learn"? (Basics of Machine Learning and Deep Learning)
Hafta 4: How Does AI "Perceive" the World? (Introduction to NLP & Computer Vision Applications)
Hafta 5: The Generative AI Revolution (LLMs, DALL-E, Prompt Engineering Basics)
Hafta 6: Ethics, Security, and Society (Bias, Transparency, Impacts on the Workforce)
Hafta 7: AI Toolbox: Practical Work with No-Code Applications (Trying what has been learned with no-code tools)
Hafta 9: AI Project Life Cycle and Management (Idea Development, Data Preparation, Model Deployment Stages)
Hafta 10: Inspiring AI Applications in Sectors (Case Studies: Finance, Health, Education, etc.)
Hafta 11: Using AI in Your Pocket: Daily Life Applications and Productivity (Personal Assistants, Filters, Recommendations)
Hafta 12: Becoming an AI-Native Organization (Organizational Transformation, Leadership, and Building an AI Strategy)
Hafta 13: Succeeding in the AI World: Strategy, Skills, and Lifelong Learning
Hafta 14: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: Major Trends, Critical Opportunities, and Career Paths
resources: Artificial Intelligence: Beyond Classical AI by Reema Thareja.
Artificial Intelligence Basics: A Non-Technical Introduction by Tom Taulli.
AI for Everyone: How to Use Artificial Intelligence to Your Advantage by Kai L. Thornton.

courses.name: Work and Employment in the 21st Century
courses.purpose: The course aims to enable students to analyze the economic, technological, and social transformations of modern working life. Students gain an analytical perspective on algorithmic management, digital labor, the future of employment, identity-based discrimination, care economy, and sustainable work models.
courses.description: This course examines the rapidly changing world of work in the 21st century from a multidimensional perspective. Its main themes include digitalization, artificial intelligence, automation, platform economy, precarity, flexibility, migration, gender, care work, sustainability, labor rights, well-being, diversity, and the structural transformation of global production networks. Cross-cutting issues such as gender, identity, migration, climate crisis, and digital transformation are integrated throughout the course.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental concepts of work and employment in the 21st century.
Analyze the effects of digitalization, artificial intelligence, and automation on the labor force.
Interpret the role of gender, identity, migration, and care work in the labor market.
Assess models of precarity, flexibility, and the platform economy.
Explain inequalities in working life and rights-based approaches.
Understand sustainable work and just transition concepts.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the concept of work in the 21st century
Hafta 3: Platform economy and gig work
Hafta 4: Gender, identity, and the labor force
Hafta 9: Workers' rights in the digital age
Hafta 10: Well-being at work, mental health
Hafta 11: Sustainable work and green transition
resources: No mandatory textbook. Weekly academic readings, reports, and case studies will be assigned.
Lecturer's handouts

courses.name: Financial Technologies
courses.purpose: In recent years, one of the most up-to-date discussions in finance and economics has been financial technologies. Many companies in the real and financial sectors have started to adapt to new digital technologies. The purpose of this course is to teach the fundamentals of fintech, the basic digital tools used in financial markets, and future developments in digital technology.
courses.description: The new digital technologies known as financial technologies are designed to enhance and automate the provision and utilization of financial services. The primary subjects associated with financial technologies are covered in this course. Many different technologies are reviewed during the course, including blockchain, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and machine learning, as well as their uses in financial markets. Additionally, this course covers crowdfunding, insurtech, regtech, new payment architectures, and upcoming developments in digital finance. Also, sustainability in the finance industry is a topic that will be discussed specifically in the last two weeks of this course.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic concepts of financial technologies
Understand how digital technologies are used in financial markets
Analyze trends in financial technologies
Interpret and predict possible future trends in digital finance
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Digital Payments and Payment Systems
Hafta 7: Investments and Technology & Robo Advisors
Hafta 9: Insurance and Technology & Risk Management
Hafta 11: Fintech Regulations and Public Policies
Hafta 12: Fintech, Money and Fiscal Policies
resources: The Future of Finance, The Impact of FinTech, AI, and Crypto on Financial Services - Henri Arslanian, Fabrice Fischer
Fintech Wars: Tech Titans, Complex Crypto and the Future of Money - James da Costa

courses.name: Globalization and Media
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the dynamic relationship between globalization and media, focusing on how media systems, industries, and technologies shape and are shaped by global cultural, political, and economic processes. Through theoretical perspectives and case studies, the course encourages students to critically engage with debates on media imperialism, cultural homogenization, hybridity, and glocalization, while also exploring the role of digital platforms, global news flows, and entertainment industries. By the end of the course, students will be able to evaluate the opportunities and challenges global media presents for identity, democracy, and cultural diversity and apply this knowledge to contemporary media practices.
courses.description: This course examines the complex relationship between globalization and media. It addresses how media systems, technologies, and content shape and are shaped by global economic, political, and cultural processes. Students will critically evaluate key theories, debates, and case studies related to media imperialism, global media flows, cultural hybridity, and the role of digital platforms in the global age.
learning_outcomes: To have knowledge about the main definitions and debates of globalization
To have a critical understanding of the history of media in the age of globalization
To understand different approaches to cultural globalization and its relationship with new media
To develop skills in global media and communication
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Globalization and Media
Hafta 5: Global Imbalance in Media Flow
Hafta 6: Network Society and Participatory Culture
Hafta 8: Digitalization and Changing Consumption Practices
Hafta 10: Presentations on Digital Consumption Analysis
Hafta 11: Introduction to the Political Economy of Media
Hafta 12: Case Studies: Migration, Diaspora and Transnational Media

courses.name: Mathematics for Data Science 2
courses.purpose: İntegrasyon teknikleri yardımıyla elemanter integrallerin integrasyonunu öğretmek. Dizilerde ve serilerde yakınsaklık kavramlarını ve bunların uygulamalarını öğretmek. Matematik bilgisini mühendislik problemlerini çözmede kullanabilme becerisi kazandırmak.
courses.description: Transandantal fonksiyonlar (ters fonksiyonlar ve türevleri, doğal logaritma, üstel fonksiyon), integrasyon teknikleri, genelleştirilmiş integraller, parametrik eğriler, kutupsal koordinatlar, kutupsal koordinatlarda integrasyon, sonsuz diziler, seriler ve kuvvet serileri.
learning_outcomes: Logaritmik, üstel, trigonometrik ve bileşik fonksiyonları, trigonometrik dönüşümler, basit kesirlere ayırma vb. integrasyon teknikleri çözmek.
Genelleştirilmiş integralleri hesaplamak ve yakınsaklıklarını belirleyebilme.
Parametrik eğrileri çizmek, eğri uzunluğu, yüzey alanı gibi hesapları yapabilme.

Dizilerin ve serilerin yakınsaklığını; kuvvet serilerinin yakınsaklık yarıçapını bulabilme becerilerini kazanır.

courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, yönetim bilgi sistemleri kavramlarını ve stratejik önemini öğretmek; bir yönetim bilgi sistemi oluşturmanın aşamaları ve bileşenleri hakkında kapsamlı bilgi kazandırmak; küresel ekonomide yönetim bilgi sistemlerinin kullanımıyla rekabet avantajı elde etme yollarını incelemek ve öğrencilerin işletme ile kuruluşların sorunlarına çözüm üretme becerisini geliştirmektir.
courses.description: Bu ders, öğrencilerin yönetim, bilişim ve sistem arasındaki etkileşimi ve yönetim bilgi sistemlerini anlamalarını sağlamak için tasarlanmıştır. Tasarım sürecinde teknolojik olarak yeniden yapılanan dünya ve yeni sanayi devrimi dikkate alınmıştır. Ders kapsamında yönetim bilişim sistemlerinin iş dünyasındaki rolü ve işletmeler ile kuruluşlar üzerindeki etkisi; yeni sanayi devrimi, veri bilimi, siber güvenlik ve iş kültüründeki değişiklikler perspektifinden ele alınmaktadır.

Sorunları çözmede ve rekabet avantajı elde etmede yönetim bilgi sisteminin rolünü anlar.

Programlama için geçerli olacak verilerin depolanması, sıralanması ve aranması gibi temel görevler ele alınacaktır. Diziler, listeler, kuyruklar, yığınlar ve ağaçlar gibi temel veri yapıları ele alınacak ve bunların çeşitli arama ve sıralama algoritmalarındaki kullanımları incelenecektir.

Pseudo dili ve akış şemalarını bilir.
Bir problemi çözmek için Pseudo dili veya akış şemalarıyla algoritmalar geliştirir.
Herhangi bir veri yapısına uygun algoritmayı uygular.
Dosyalama algoritmaları geliştirir.

courses.name: Management and Organization in Digital World

courses.name: Introduction to Data Science
courses.purpose: Bu ders, öğrencilere R programlamaya, veri düzenlemeye (data wrangling), keşifsel veri analizine (EDA) ve temel istatistiksel modellemeye giriş sağlar. Öğrenciler temel R fonksiyonlarını öğrenerek gerçek hayat veri setleri üzerinde analiz tekniklerini uygulayacaktır.

learning_outcomes: Veri, enformasyon, bilgi kavramları arasındaki farkı bilir ve bunları örneklerle tanımlar.
Yapılandırılmış veri, yapılandırılmamış veri ve yarı-yapılandırılmış verinin ne olduğunu bilir.
Verinin toplanması ve organize edilmesi süreçlerindeki adımları açıklar.
Temel istatistiki analizleri bir veri analiz dili yardımıyla uygular.
Bir veri seti üzerinde regresyon tabanlı bir analiz gerçekleştirir ve elde edilen sonuçları yorumlar.

Hafta 2: Temel R Programlama • Veri tipleri ve veri yapıları • exp() dahil matematiksel fonksiyonlar
Hafta 3: Veri İçe Aktarma • CSV dosyalarını okuma • head(), str(), summary() kullanımı

Hafta 7: Keşifsel Veri Analizi (EDA) • Özet istatistikler • Dağılım analizleri
Hafta 8: Temel İstatistiksel Modelleme • Bağımlı ve bağımsız değişkenler
Hafta 9: lm() ile Doğrusal Modelleme • Doğrusal model oluşturma ve yorumlama

courses.name: Introduction to LLM and Generative AI Applications
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental principles of Large Language Models (LLMs) and generative AI technologies, explain how these technologies work, and demonstrate their applications across different fields with examples. It also aims to provide students with a basic understanding of model training, usage, and ethical implications, while enabling them to analyze current generative AI tools such as ChatGPT, DALL·E, and Codex and gain hands-on experience through applied projects.
courses.description: The course begins with the fundamentals of artificial intelligence and machine learning, then covers the structure, training processes, and use cases of large language models (LLMs). It examines generative AI applications such as text generation, image creation, and code generation through models like ChatGPT, DALL·E, and Codex. Deep learning architectures, especially the transformer structure, ethical issues, misinformation generation, and responsible use of AI are also evaluated. Practical sessions encourage students to experience generative AI tools and develop simple projects.
learning_outcomes: Has a broad interdisciplinary perspective, especially related to business and computer engineering, in management information systems.
Understands management information systems from technical, organizational, and managerial perspectives and uses a current programming language by knowing programming logic.
Uses different information technologies and systems for understanding and solving various business problems.
Interprets data, concepts, and ideas in the field of management information systems using scientific and technological methods.
Analyzes the requirements of an information system and masters the processes in the analysis, design, and implementation stages of the system database.
Contributes technically and managerially to information projects and takes responsibility.
Solves complex business and information problems using various statistical techniques and numerical methods, and performs analyses effectively using statistical software.
Uses a foreign language at least at B1 General Level according to the European Language Portfolio criteria.
Develops teamwork, negotiation, leadership, and entrepreneurship skills.
Has universal ethical values, social responsibility awareness, and sufficient legal knowledge.
Develops positive attitudes toward lifelong learning, identifies individual learning needs, and works to meet them.
Communicates ideas and solution proposals related to the field both in written and oral form, and presents and publishes them when necessary in national and international platforms.
Uses computer software and information and communication technologies at an advanced level equivalent to the European Computer Driving Licence required by the field.

Hafta 2: Fundamentals of Deep Learning and Neural Networks

Hafta 4: Large Language Models: ChatGPT, GPT-3, GPT-4
Hafta 5: Generative AI Concept and Application Areas
Hafta 6: Text Generation: Prompt Design and Interaction Techniques
Hafta 7: Image Generation: DALL·E, Midjourney and Similar Models

Hafta 15: Final Project Presentations and Feedback

courses.name: Operational Supply Chain Management
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental principles of supply chain management (SCM) and its strategic importance in business operations.
Analyze supply chain structures, network design, and optimization strategies to improve efficiency.
Apply demand forecasting models and inventory management techniques to minimize costs and meet customer demands.
Evaluate procurement processes and supplier relationship management to ensure a smooth supply flow.
Compare different logistics and transportation strategies for cost-effective and timely deliveries.
Apply digital innovations in SCM such as Artificial Intelligence, Internet of Things, Blockchain, and automation to enhance supply chain transparency and efficiency.
Identify risks in global supply chains and develop resilience and crisis management strategies.
Evaluate sustainability challenges in supply chain management and propose green supply chain practices to reduce environmental impact.
Develop problem-solving and analytical skills through real-world case studies and supply chain improvement projects.
Present supply chain strategies and solutions effectively through reports and business presentations.

courses.name: İstinyelilik Manifestosu 4
courses.purpose: Lisans öğrencilerinin üniversite yaşamına adaptasyonlarını sağlamak, üniversite hayatları boyunca sosyal ve akademik açıdan bir altyapı oluşturmak ve yetkinlik gelişimlerini desteklemek. Liderlik ve sosyal etkiyi geliştirmek için düşünme, yenilikçilik, esneklik ve adaptasyon konularında sosyal becerilere odaklanmak.
courses.description: Bu program; analitik düşünme ve inovasyon, zihinsel süreçlerin çalıştırılmasıyla karmaşık problem çözme yetileri, yaratıcılık, özgünlük, girişimcilik, teknoloji kullanımı izleme ve kontrolü, eleştirel düşünme ve analiz edebilme becerilerini geliştirecek akademik yetkinlikler konularında seminerler sunar.

courses.name: E Commerce
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, öğrencilere çağdaş dijital ekonomi bağlamında e-ticaretin gelişimi, yapısı ve stratejik yönetimi konusunda kapsamlı bir bilgi ve bakış açısı kazandırmaktır. Ders kapsamında; dijital iş stratejileri, tüketici davranışları, çevrimiçi pazarlama uygulamaları, siber güvenlik, yasal düzenlemeler, veri analitiği ile yapay zekâ ve otomasyon gibi gelişen teknolojilere ilişkin temel ve kritik bilgiler ele alınmaktadır. Bu çerçevede ders, öğrencileri hem yerel hem de küresel düzeyde rekabet edebilen, etik değerlere bağlı ve müşteri odaklı e-ticaret çözümleri geliştirebilen stratejik karar vericiler olarak yetiştirmeyi amaçlamaktadır. Öğrencilerin dijital araçları, veri analitiğini ve yenilikçi teknolojileri etkin, bilinçli ve stratejik bir biçimde kullanma yetkinliği kazanmaları hedeflenmektedir.
courses.description: Bu ders, e-ticaret alanındaki temel kavram ve uygulamaları kapsamaktadır. Ders kapsamında; küresel pazarlarda e-ticaretin gelişimi ve dönüşümü, e-ticaret iş modelleri, dijital ödeme sistemleri, siber güvenlik ve yasal uyum süreçleri ele alınmaktadır. Ayrıca SEO, sosyal medya ve etkileyici (influencer) pazarlama stratejileri, çevrimiçi tüketici verilerinin analizi, web ve mobil platformlarda müşteri odaklı dijital deneyim tasarımı, e-ticarette yapay zekâ araçları ve otomasyon teknikleri gibi güncel konular da incelenmektedir.
learning_outcomes: E-ticaretin gelişimini, işleyişini ve dijital dönüşüm sürecini tanımlar ve açıklar.
İş modellerini (B2B, B2C, C2C, D2C), gelir yapıları ve rekabet stratejilerini açıklar.
Platformlar, ödeme sistemleri, güvenlik mekanizmaları ve dijital pazarlama kanalları dâhil olmak üzere e-ticaret ekosisteminin bileşenlerini tanımlar.
Tüketici davranışı, SEO ilkeleri, müşteri analitiği, sosyal medya stratejileri, oyunlaştırma, yapay zekâ uygulamaları ve yeni teknolojileri açıklar.
Dijital ticareti etkileyen teknolojik, hukuki, etik ve küresel eğilimleri analiz eder.
Geleneksel ticaret ile dijital ticaret modellerini karşılaştırır.
KPI’ları, çevrimiçi satın alma davranışlarını, siber güvenlik risklerini ve dijital dönüşüm verilerini yorumlar.
Artırılmış gerçeklik (AR), sanal gerçeklik (VR), blokzincir, sesli ticaret ve otomasyon araçları gibi yeni teknolojileri e-ticaret bağlamında sınıflandırır.
E-ticaret iş modelleri ve rekabet stratejileri geliştirmek için stratejik planlama araçlarını uygular.
Marka kimliği, içerik mimarisi ve kullanıcı odaklı arayüzler içeren işlevsel bir e-ticaret varlığı tasarlar ve uygular.
Bölümlendirme, hedefleme, SEO ve sosyal medya stratejileri kullanarak dijital pazarlama kampanyaları geliştirir.
Tüketici davranışı analizi yapar ve müşteri analitiği panellerini yorumlar.
Güvenli ödeme sistemlerini uygular ve siber güvenlik iyi uygulamalarını hayata geçirir.
Oyunlaştırılmış etkileşim mekanizmaları ve yapay zekâ destekli kişiselleştirme süreçleri geliştirir.
Pazar eğilimleri, performans göstergeleri ve dijital dönüşüm sonuçlarını analiz eden profesyonel raporlar hazırlar.
Yeni teknolojiler ve otomasyon araçlarını entegre eden geleceğe yönelik stratejiler oluşturur.
İş dünyasında teknolojik yenilik ve dijital dönüşüme değer verir.
Kullanıcı odaklı tasarım, erişilebilirlik standartları ve kapsayıcı dijital uygulamaların önemini benimser.
Etik ilkelere, yasal düzenlemelere ve sorumlu veri kullanımına bağlılık gösterir.
Tüketici gizliliği, siber güvenlik riskleri ve küresel ekonomik dalgalanmalara karşı duyarlılık geliştirir.
Dijital pazarlama, influencer iş birlikleri ve yapay zekâ uygulamalarında etik farkındalık sergiler.
Sürdürülebilir, sorumlu ve şeffaf dijital ticaret anlayışını destekler.
İş stratejisi, pazarlama, teknoloji, analitik ve inovasyonu bütüncül e-ticaret çözümlerine entegre eder.
Müşteri içgörüleri, güvenlik unsurları ve pazar eğilimlerini sentezleyerek stratejik çözümler geliştirir.
Dijital performans göstergelerini değerlendirir ve kurumsal sonuçları iyileştirmeye yönelik bilinçli kararlar alır.
Dijital dönüşüm girişimlerini yönetir ve farklı fonksiyonlar arasında koordinasyon sağlar.
Teknolojik değişimlere ve dönüşen tüketici davranışlarına uyum sağlar.
Dijital inovasyon süreçlerine liderlik eder ve çok disiplinli ekiplerle etkin iş birliği yapar.
E-ticaret operasyonlarını tasarlama, yönetme ve optimize etme konusunda mesleki yetkinlik gösterir.
Güvenli, veri odaklı ve teknoloji destekli iş ortamlarında etkin biçimde çalışır.
İşletme, pazarlama, bilişim, analitik ve hukuk gibi disiplinler arası alanlarda edindiği bilgileri uygular.
Etik, hukuki ve stratejik dijital iş kararlarında sorumluluk üstlenir.
Veri temelli stratejik girişimlerle kurumsal performansa katkı sağlar.
Sürekli değişen e-ticaret ortamında rekabetçi kalabilmek için yaşam boyu öğrenme yaklaşımını benimser.
Dinamik dijital pazarlarda bağımsız ve profesyonel şekilde faaliyet gösterir.

courses.name: Information Technology Strategies and Management
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the strategic role of Information Technology (IT) in business management. The course will focus on how IT can be used to enhance competitive advantage, support business strategies, and facilitate decision making. Furthermore, it aims to develop students' managerial skills in the effective and efficient use of IT resources.
courses.description: 1. Overview of IT strategies and their role in business management. 2. Analysis of IT in the context of strategic planning and decision making. 3. Examination of the management of IT resources, including human resources, budgeting, and operations. 4. Investigation of the impact of IT on organizational structure and competitiveness. 5. Discussion on the ethical, legal, and social issues related to IT management and strategies.
learning_outcomes: BT stratejilerinin işletme yönetimindeki rolünü anlamak.
İş kararlarında BT'nin stratejik kullanımını analiz edebilecektir.
BT kaynaklarını etkin ve verimli bir şekilde yönetmek.
BT'nin iş rekabetçiliği üzerindeki etkisini değerlendirir.
BT yönetimi ve strateji geliştirmede etik, yasal ve sosyal konuları ele alır.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to IT strategies and their role in business management.
Hafta 6: BT yönetiminde yasal ve etik hususlar.
Hafta 7: Gözden geçirme ve ara sınav için hazırlık.
Hafta 9: Karar vermede BT'nin stratejik kullanımı.
Hafta 15: Gözden geçirme ve final sınavına hazırlık.

courses.name: Introduction to Operating Systems
courses.purpose: İşletim sistemlerinin temel kavramlarını anlamak. Öğrencileri bir işletim sisteminin farklı bileşenleri ve işlevleriyle tanıştırmak. Çeşitli işletim sistemi türlerini ve özelliklerini keşfetmek. İşletim sistemi kavramlarıyla ilgili problem çözme ve eleştirel düşünme becerilerini geliştirmek. Uygulamalı alıştırmalar ve projeler yoluyla pratik deneyim kazanmak.
courses.description: İşletim sistemlerine giriş, bellek yönetimi, süreç yönetimi, eşzamanlı süreçler, kilitlenmeler, işlemci yönetimi, I/O ve cihaz yönetimi, dosya yönetimi ve dosya sistemleri, dağıtık işletim sistemlerine giriş, dağıtık sistemlerde senkronizasyon, dağıtılmış dosya sistemleri, çağdaş bakış işletim sistemi teknolojisi.
learning_outcomes: Bir işletim sisteminin temel fonksiyonlarını ve bileşenlerini tanımlar.
Süreç yönetimi ve çizelgeleme tekniklerini anlayın.
Bellek yönetimi kavramlarını ve sanal belleği açıklar.
Dosya sistemlerini ve depolama yönetimini tartışın.
Farklı işletim sistemi türlerini ve özelliklerini tanımlar.
Gerçek dünyadaki sorunları çözmek için işletim sistemi kavramlarını uygulayın.
İşletim sistemi araçlarını ve yardımcı programlarını kullanma konusunda pratik beceriler geliştirin.

courses.name: Social Media Analytics
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to collect, analyze, and interpret data from various social media platforms. By the end of the course, students should be able to use social media analytics tools, understand the impact of social media data on business decisions, and generate actionable insights for strategic planning.

learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamentals of social media analytics
Collect and analyze data from various social media platforms
Use social media analytics tools efficiently
Interpret social media data and generate actionable business insights
Apply knowledge gained through case studies to real-world scenarios

courses.name: Digital Transformation
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı öğrencilere dijital dönüşüm kavramı ve bunun iş ortamındaki etkileri hakkında derinlemesine bir anlayış sağlamaktır. Ders, öğrencilere dijital dönüşümde kullanılan çeşitli strateji ve modelleri, verinin, analitiğin ve teknolojinin rolünü ve bununla ilgili etik, sosyal ve güvenlik konularını tanıtmayı amaçlamaktadır. Ders aynı zamanda öğrencilere farklı sektörlerden gerçek dünya örnek olay incelemelerini sunarak onlara pratik bilgiler sunacaktır.
courses.description: Dijital dönüşüm kavramını anlamaya yönelik teori ve uygulama, dijital dönüşüm stratejileri ve modelleri, verinin, analitiğin ve teknolojinin dijital dönüşümdeki rolü, farklı sektörlerde dijital dönüşüm örnek olayları ile etik, sosyal ve güvenlik konuları işlenmektedir.
learning_outcomes: Dijital dönüşümün teorisi ve pratiğine bakış.
Dijital dönüşümde verinin, analitiğin ve teknolojinin rolünün anlaşılması.
Dijital dönüşümde çeşitli strateji ve modellerin bilgisi.
Dijital dönüşümde etik, sosyal ve güvenlik konularına ilişkin farkındalık.
Dijital dönüşümde vaka çalışmalarını analiz edebilme.

courses.name: Current Topics in Management Information System
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, teknolojideki en son trendleri ve başarılı uygulama alanlarını, farklı teknolojiler arasındaki ilişkileri ve birbirlerini nasıl etkilediklerini, yeni teknolojilerin dijital dönüşümü nasıl tetiklediğini ve iş ve günlük yaşamı nasıl etkilediğini keşfetmektir. Öğrenciler, iş yaşamında ve yönetimde trendleri ve ortaya çıkan teknolojileri etkili ve verimli bir şekilde nasıl değerlendireceklerini öğrenecek ve kuruluşlarının bu yeni teknolojileri iş süreçlerinde ve çalışmalarında tanımlamasına, seçmesine ve uygulamasına yardımcı olacak. Bu ders, öğrencilere teknolojide yeni yönler ve onların iş çevreleri için önemlerini ve uygulanabilirliklerini ve ayrıca geliştirdikleri yeni zorlukları ve sorunları değerlendirmek için etkili yöntemler sunar. Gelişmekte olan teknolojileri ve hangi sorunları ele aldıkları ve özel olarak en son eğilimleri ve uygulamaları öğreneceklerdir.
courses.description: Ders kapsamında dünyayı, günlük yaşamı ve iş süreçlerini etkileyen yeni teknolojilerin yanı sıra başarılı uygulama alanları ve vaka çalışmaları tanıtılacak; bu tür teknolojilerin farklı endüstriler üzerindeki etkisi vurgulanacaktır. Amaç, öğrencinin bu yeni teknolojilerin doğru yöntemler kullanılarak iş süreçlerine ve organizasyona nasıl entegre edilebileceğini anlamak ve böylece farklılaşmak ve rekabet avantajı elde etmektir. Yeni teknolojilerin durumu ve iş modellerinde veya planlarında nasıl değerlendirilmesi ve konumlandırılması gerektiği ve yeni teknolojilerin uygulanmasındaki metodolojiler sunulacaktır.
learning_outcomes: Yapay zeka, nesnelerin interneti, mobil iletişim, büyük veri, analitik, dijital pazarlama, robotik, giyilebilir cihazlar, bulut bilişim, artırılmış gerçeklik ve sanal gerçeklik gibi gelişmekte olan teknolojileri incelemek ve değerlendirmek.
Yeni teknolojilerin iş süreçleri ve çalışma ortamı üzerindeki etkisini anlamak.
Dijital teknolojilerdeki en önemli genel eğilimleri ve bu eğilimlerin ekonomik ve sosyal etkilerini birleştirmek.
Yeni teknolojilerin başarılı bir şekilde benimsenmesini etkileyen kritik faktörleri belirlemek.
Yeni teknolojilerin temel özelliklerini, iş faydalarını, risklerini ve maliyet yönlerini tanımak.
Önümüzdeki dönemde yeni teknolojilerin seçimini şu anda etkileyen veya etkilemesi muhtemel olan teknoloji eğilimlerini tanımlamak.

Genel iş stratejisindeki teknoloji trendlerinin işlevini değerlendirin ve bu trendlerin her departmanda nasıl değerlendirilmesi gerektiğini incelemek.
Yeni teknolojilerin kullanımındaki araçların kuruluşların yetkinlikleriyle bütünleşmesine yardımcı olmak ve müşteri deneyimi oluşturmadaki stratejileri ana hatlarıyla belirtmek.

Bu kursta, çeşitli farklı grafikler oluşturmak için ggplot2'yi nasıl kullanacağınız öğretilir. Ek olarak, ggplot2'nin farklı uzantıları ve R'deki etkileşimli grafikler tartışılacaktır.

courses.name: Big Data Storaging Technologies
courses.purpose: Bu dersin temel amacı öğrencilere Büyük Veri Depolama Teknolojileri hakkında kapsamlı bir anlayış kazandırmaktır.
